top_profane.text:
So I figured I would send you guys/gals/masters of tiny finger tool blacksmithery a little note of appreciation for making a quality product any dude would be happy to show off in his already over masculinized bathroom (totally not true, my wife won't let me make it look manly, but..... gotta pick your battles).

For years mankind has been plagued with a never-ending threat. A threat so ever-present in our daily lives we almost never think about it, care about it, or pretend to care about it.... until it strikes! ....... hangnails. Yup, hangnails. Oh don't pretend like they don't bother you or you're too much of a stud to care... They destroy you and YOU KNOW IT! And they usually start with a crappy ass nail clip job. They can literally stop a person dead in their tracks, unrelenting in their tiny little annoying pain that grown ass men have groveled beneath their might. One wouldn't dare just rip it off! Oh No.... that could make it worse. Then the pain could increase, oh God it could get horrible, on par with that stupid little sliver you got from your cheap ass Walmart fiberglass tent pole 2 years ago setting the stupid tent up in your backyard cause little Jimmy just HAD to go camping that night, and burying itself into the side of your pinky finger, still letting itself be known every time you graze your hand across a piece of clothing.... but good luck finding that little triumph of joy to pull it out....... yeah, that kind of pain.

Hangnail pain can be like that. Even better is when you realize you did it to yourself earlier and without warning with your crappy ass nail clippers that never cut well and always leave your nail jagged and prone to snagging.... on everything. Even better still when you realize you had to search for 20 minutes to find said crappy nail clippers, the whole time reminding yourself that 'this' time you'll buy a new set when you hit the store. 20 minutes later you curse and give up and buy a new set cause you had no luck finding the clippers in your super organized man life that you pride yourself so well on (you even looked in your tool-box, next to the torque wrench, cause... well.... you drank a lot of beer yesterday working on the lawn mower and maybe it was a good idea to put the clippers in the tool box after using them as snap ring removal devices, after all.... they are "tools"). Brand new clippers.... $2.99 top shelf shiny silver clippers with the bad-ass nail file and pokey wanna-be knife thingy inside.... these are gonna rock! But storm clouds are a brewin' my firends, trust me.

First you cut yourself on the crappy blister pack packaging trying to open them, and not like a little scrape/drag abrasion either. I'm talking one of those deep Hellacious ones in the crease of your index finger that is sure to not heal for weeks to come, potentially matching levels of pain similar to the fiberglass sliver and the hangnail your are presently attempting to hack from existence. Well hack away my friends, cause you soon realize your super elevated top shelf $2.99 clippers cut about as good as those safety scissor you were handed in 2nd grade to ensure you never draw blood, it's too late. You've just created another hangnail..... maybe not immediately, but it'll show itself in a few days, without fail. But wait... no big deal... I've got this sweet bad-ass nail file and pokey wanna-be knife thing inside to fix any downfall of these ultra shiny wonderments of finger-care. After all, the entire clipper couldn't suck right? You just got a weird pair and need to get used to them. Yeah.... not gonna work. They cut like crap, they nail file would be better used as an actual nail, the pokey wanna-be knife thing is the only usable piece on the entire clipper.... and that's only cause it literally has to imitate a stick to dig gunk out from under your nail! I mean honestly this thing is probably worse than the so-said imitated stick... cause at least a stick doesn't pretend to be anything other than a stick!

Anywho.... so you play this game about 12 more times over the next couple years until your wife surprises you with a little yellow box one day.... labeled Klipit. Ignoring the fact that this is the first time someone has gifted you nail clippers, you laugh about the whole thing and give her funny looks. But wait, where is the normal blister pack packaging? These can't really be clippers can they? Oh buddy, hold onto your ass for this one!!!! First off, high quality packaging. Not even like normal high quality, I mean like ultra top end, packaging engineering style top end, the type that you actually kinda feel good about having opposable thumbs when you open it, it's actually satisfying to hold in your hand 'when' you open it. Quality written all over it right there. Then out of the package comes not one... oh no, TWO clippers! your standard sized normal fits in your hand finger clippers, then it's jumbo big brother capable of clipping off an entire big toe! HOLY CLIPPERS OF FREEDOM BATMAN! Now THIS is what I'm talking about! They are even nicer than my torque wrench! Nice manly satin finish, no overly shiny, get blinded by the new LED lights in the ceiling from the reflection finish for this guy. Just pure well machined and well finished products of awesome. But, why is there blue tape on the han...... OOooohhhhh, nail file... right.... pbbbttthh, like those ever work...... HOLY CRAP I COULD SAND GLASS WITH THIS THING! It actually sands my nail, and sands it good, like seriously be careful kiddies... unheard of in clippers until now. Oh boy howdy, these are the shizzle right here, and I bet the wanna be knife thing is actually a knife!..... oh wait, there isn't one.... DAMNIT.... so close to perfection. Oh well, they come with a sweet yellow storage case so you can easily find them in the tool drawer now! Move over torque wrench, daddy has a new favorite! They fit right into man hands without slipping or sliding out when actuating the jaws of clipper purity, seriously these things clip so well I dare say they beat the wire cutters I resorted to using after so many useless $2.99 clippers failed to keep hangnails from plaguing my otherwise perfect life. The jaws open plenty wide for my weird ass man feet nails, and plenty of torque on that tiny moment arm for clipping right through the nail/wannabe epoxy-like substance I call a nail. But enough about me... let's continue on our path of Klipit enlightenment.... by means of pro's and con's....

Pro's:
- Manly/masculine design with appealing satin finished highly machined body, and quality logo on top.
- Two perfectly formed jaws of cutting doom that will destroy all nail and nail-like material in its path. Like.... really sharp here people.
- Well positioned and impressively functional nail file formed right into the handle.
- Very nice packaging creating a pleasurable if not ethereal sensation upon opening.
- YOU GET TWO CLIPPERS!!! nomal ones and giant PI$$-OFF clippers that'll further destroy all nail and nail-like material in its path.
- Sending you guys a desperate note for another under-appreciated set of clippers to keep in the bathroom cause I lost the first set in my tool box.... Didn't keep them in the yellow package for safe keeping..... but I still have my torque wrench, so go figure.
- Haven't taken them apart yet (I'm kinda known for doing that), but I'm sure they will be just as pleasurable to tinker with as they are to clip with.
- Showing them off at parties!
- Why are you still reading the Pro list, isn't this enough for a nail clipper?

Con's:
- No wanna-be knife thingy for digging gunk from under your nails, or removing the snap-ring holding your wrist-pin to the connecting rod of your lawn mower engine.
- Feeling like you should show them off at parties!
- Ummm...... "KHHHHAAAAANNNN".... as said by Capt. Kirk.... I really don't have any other cons....

So.... In conclusion. Thank you Harperton for providing the world with the Klipit nail clipper set. They cut very well, very cleanly, and very satisfyingly. I appreciate the dedication to quality, function, and appearance. Also another shout-out on the packaging that doesn't cut your hand when you over-aggressively rip it open. Nice touch. And thank you for providing me the opportunity to share my story with you in hopes of obtaining a another set. Pretty damn cool of you Harperton!

Sincerely,
--PK from the sunny western beaches of Idaho.
top_profane.title: Clippers fit for a KING!...or the king we wish we all were...or for tiny cloven hoofed animals, cause these things are LEGIT!

top_profane.text:
Okay, here's what you need to know about me to understand what a big deal this 5-star review is:

1) I have crap hair. Seriously. I know women always say they have crap hair, but I really do. My mom had baby-fine, very curly hair, and my dad had baby-fine very straight hair. So I have baby-fine hair that is curly underneath and straight on top. Even my stylist says that I have the most "challenging" hair she's ever worked with. ("Challenging" is her nice way of not saying "crappy".) Even my husband, who loves me very much, says he thinks I'm beautiful but he understands why I get frustrated with my hair. Seriously, it's a complete mess.

2) I'm 46 years old. That means I've been dealing with this crappy hair with more than three decades. I have tried everything: every product, every dryer, curler, straightener, brush, every cut -- with bangs, without, long, short, etc. You name it. I've tried it to make my hair less crappy. I have some skin in the game, and I'm no rookie.

3) As much as I have struggled with my hair, I am lazy at heart, and I DREAM of having hair I don't have to spend ridiculous amounts of time on just to make it look kind of normal. What I want, more than anything, is something that will be easy, and make my hair look like it's not a frizzy ball of crap. Any successes I have had were minimal at best, barring a product or two that at least helped me tame the beast.

So. Now we come to the Infinitipro styler. Oh. My. GOD, does my hair look fantastic right now.

I just got this thing a couple of days ago, and I cannot believe how fantastic it is. Friends, this is a revelation. I seriously can't believe it. I was sure the bristles would be too soft and would make my hair flyaway and staticky. Nope. I was sure that it would take forever to dry my hair with this thing. Nope, it took me less time than my dryer and brush -- by a long shot. Now, the one caveat is that I did use my blow dryer to blow most of the moisture out of my hair first. But barring that, the Infinitipro dried my hair in about half of the time that I generally take in the styling phase. My hair looks glossy, it is doing what I want it to do, and I didn't have to struggle with a dryer and a brush at the same time. I haven't used the spin function yet because I've wanted my hair straight, but I think I will probably try that this weekend for a look with more volume.

I would recommend the Infinitipro to anyone with fine hair, especially someone with shoulder-length hair like mine. I just can't say enough about it. I even canceled a haircut appointment I had for today because my hair looks so good right now that I'm able to think about growing it longer for the first time in years. So, who knows, maybe it will even save me money on haircuts in the long run? :-)
top_profane.title: Oh. My. God.

top_profane.text:
This was REALLY frustrating…

First, I’ll give some quick notes I learned while putting it together so that you can quickly see them all in one place and don’t have to sludge through the rest of my review to see them. Then, I’ll give my in depth review if you want to read that.

QUICK NOTES FOR PUTTING IT TOGETHER:
⚫️The screws aren’t in a bag anywhere. They are in each end of the metal frames (total of 8 screws, 4 on each metal frame).
⚫️Mine didn’t come with the hexagon key it was supposed to come with. It only came with the small flat wrench. So, you might need your own screwdriver, but I’d recommend that anyways because for me screwdrivers are always easier than those darn hex keys!
⚫️ The instruction sheet doesn’t state which end of the side panels is up and which is down. I figured it out by putting my finger in the poles of the side panels. The ends that you could feel the threading in are the bottoms because you screw the wheels in there.
⚫️The 2 metal frames go between the side panels to hold them together. One goes at the top and one at the bottom. One metal frame is shaped kind of like a long rectangle that has 4 ends poking out the same distance, the other is kind of an H shape with 2 lines in the middle of the H. The H shaped goes on TOP, the other one goes on BOTTOM.
⚫️When you put one of the metal frames on and go to put the other one on, if it seems like there isn’t holes in the pole to attach the frame to, it could just be the side panels slid and separated so they’re covering it. Make sure to slide them back together snugly and that will make room to show the holes to attach the metal frame to
⚫️ The top tray can be secured. The easiest way is to have the top 2 drawers out. Then you can see underneath the top tray there are kind of latches with screws to secure them in place.

IN DEPTH REVIEW…

Overall, it was extremely frustrating to put together! Someone better at these things might not find it frustrating at all! Hopefully my review will help those like me who need more than the crappy instruction sheet that was included with this! I think when charging a whopping $149.99 (at the time of writing this review), they could manage to give more detailed instructions! Honestly, I really like the cart, but it’s nowhere near worth $149.99 in my opinion! It’s all plastic aside from the wheels, 2 metal frames, and the “heavy duty thickened aluminum tray.” In my opinion there isn’t anything that makes it worth more than $40 at most! The crap instructions and how it needed cleaned isn’t something I want if I’m going to spend $150 on something. It’s durable and sturdy for a plastic cart, but it’s still a plastic cart. It seems way overpriced. I did a quick Google search for other “Color Mixing Salon Trolley Carts” and found way better looking ones for $100-130. Carts similar to this were in the $40-50 range. So, I really wouldn’t recommend going with this one. But, if you don’t mind the price, it works well once it’s put together. I don’t like rating things based on price since we all have different ideas on what’s affordable/expensive. But, given this seems to be so extremely overpriced I am taking a star off for it. I also am taking a star off for the horrible instructions and for the screws being in the metal frames without saying that on the crap instruction sheet and for it being a little dirty and needing wiped down. So, I give it 3 stars.

It came inside an Amazon box that was HUUUUUUGE! I live in an apartment building and the box was left downstairs near the mailboxes. When I saw it I didn’t think it was mine because I didn’t order anything that big. Of course, it was mine. Luckily, over half the box was filled with paper to make up for all the leftover room. I get Amazon wants the items in Amazon packaging, but what is so hard about finding a box that actually fits the item without having to fill A TON of leftover room with paper or air bags?! Amazon is notorious for sending items in boxes WAY TOO BIG. Anyways…inside the huge box was another shipping box with labels for when that shipped I’m guessing to Amazon.

The stuff was packed nicely in the box. I took everything out to see if I had it all. There’s an instruction sheet…if you want to call it that. It’s only about 2% helpful. It showed 2 screws on the instruction sheet. You need 8. I looked all over to find a bag with screws. There was a bag with the 4 wheels in it so I figured the screws would be in there…nope. There was a bag with the “heavy duty thickened aluminum tray” so I thought maybe they’d be in there…nope. There wasn’t anywhere else they’d be since the drawers were just in plastic covers too, but I checked anyways…nope. Looked all throughout the box to make sure I didn’t miss anything…nope. So, I thought, great, no screws. Then, I finally noticed on the 2 square metal frame things they had the 8 screws in each one. WHY?! It’s not like it helped make things easier. I then had to REMOVE them to be able to screw the metal frames onto the cart. And of course, some of them weren’t put in straight so they were a pain to get out. It was really frustrating. So, just know, look for the screws there.

There was apparently supposed to be a hexagon key (according to the instruction sheet) and a small really thin wrench. I got the small wrench, but didn’t get a hexagon key. If I was only getting one, I would have rather gotten the hexagon key since I needed that more than the small wrench that was only used for the wheels. But, whatever, I got out my screwdriver.

It comes with the 2 side panels, the drawers and top part all separate, the wheels separate, and the metal frames separate.

The 2 metal frames on the instruction sheet look completely different from the ones I got. On the instruction sheet, it looks like there’s one that’s perfect square and another that is square too but looks like of the 4 ends that poke out, one end on each side comes out more. That’s not how mine were. I had one that was a long rectangle that had the 4 ends poking out all poking out the same distance. The other was kind of like an H shape with 2 lines in the middle of the H. You can see what I mean in my photos.

The instruction sheet also didn’t say how you were supposed to determine which end was the top of each panel. I felt the inside of each pole and noticed that inside the ones on one end had where something could be screwed into them and the other ends didn’t. So, I determined the ends that could have something screwed in were the bottoms since the wheels would be screwed into them.

Now, you take one of the metal frames and use it to connect the 2 side panels to it. Like I said, I had no clue which metal frame went where since mine didn’t look like the ones on the instruction sheet. I put both in the bottom area and it seemed like only 1 fit, so I went with that one. Screwed it in. Then went to put the other metal frame on the top….and found out that wouldn’t work. So, I had it wrong somehow and ended up having to unscrew the metal frame I had put on the bottom since that was apparently the one that went on the top. For your reference, the H shaped one goes on top and the other goes on the bottom.

While you’re trying to handle the panels and screw in the metal frames, the side panels keep sliding up and down on the poles, which gets super annoying!

When I finally got the right metal frame on the bottom and went to put the right one on the top, I thought I did it wrong and did have it right the first time because it seemed like there wasn’t a hole on the inside of the pole to put the screws and attach the metal frame. It seemed like when I was screwing in the bottom metal frame maybe the poles moved and that was why. But, it turned out with the side panels constantly sliding up and down, they separated some and needed to be slid back together, which made room at the top and showed the holes for the metal frame to attach to…thank goodness because I probably would have given up if I had to start that all over 😂

Once you get the 2 metal frames on and attach the wheels, the only thing left to do was slide in the drawers, sit the top one on the top part of the cart, and attach the “heavy duty thickened aluminum tray.” I wasn’t going to use the aluminum tray so just put that aside in case I ever change my mind in the future.

I thought it was kind of dumb that the top part just sits on top and had no way of being secured so it could easily detach if someone grabbed it or bumped it even. So then I thought maybe I could figure a way to secure it myself. But, I realized underneath there was a way to secure it. So, I removed the top 2 drawers and you can see there’s screws and a kind of latch. You move the latch to go over and screw it in to secure the top part. I felt a lot better knowing that was secure on there!

The wheels work well. They roll on hard floor and carpet great without getting stuck at all.

The “heavy duty thickened aluminum tray” isn’t even what I’d call a tray. It’s literally a thin tube of aluminum swirled around to hold something like a hairdryer or something. I don’t even know how it’s supposed to really go in the hole and stay in place very well since it seems like you just kind of stick it in there?

The top drawer doesn’t have that space, but I don’t mind it. The drawers slide in and out easily, maybe a little too easily. You can slide them in and out from both sides, so that’s nice.

The top tray has 2 round areas and 1 large square area to place things. It also has 2 round holes and 2 thinner kind of rounded rectangular holes. So, you can place things in those spots too. I like that it has space on 2 sides that the drawers can sit in. I’m not sure how well they’ll hang there if you had a lot in the drawer, but they work well without too much in the drawers.

The product page says “the front dust baffle is exquisite and durable.” I have no clue what a dust baffle is. I can tell you it does get dusty because it came pretty dusty and seemed to have a bunch of thin metal pieces in it. It looked like dust but had reflection like metal dust pieces if you know what I mean. So I had to wipe down the whole thing - inside the drawers, inside and outside both side panels, etc.

top_profane.text:
I got fed up with crappy clippers a while back, and I hit Amazon to pick up a premium pair. To hedge my bets, I ordered two: these, and the [[ASIN:B009WUA658 Tweezerman Deluxe Nail Clipper Set]]. I'm not going to lie: I'm a sucker for USA made stuff, and that's why I bought these. As much as I hate to admit it, I bought the Tweezerman set just in case these sucked because I trusted the brand, and I thought it was a surefire win.

Long story short: These put the Tweezerman to SHAME, and that's not to say the Tweezerman are a bad clipper by any measure -- quite the contrary, if I had opened them before this No-Mes, clipper, they would have (briefly) been the best I'd ever used. But these, well, they're just amazing.

I was beginning to think that I had nails made out of some x-men, superhuman supermaterial. I've seriously broken three pairs of clippers in the past year - two of them the 'hook' on the centerpost snapped off, and the third gave way at the bend for the cutting head. I've always had to crush my clippers with what seemed like an excessive amount of force, and in the end I'd be rewarded with a ragged, but shorter nail. No more- these cut like butter. I thought that maybe my nails were just a bit soft this time, so I tried my old set on the same nail, and it still needed tons of force. I also tried the Tweezerman clipper, and while it was extremely good, it still didn't quite match up to these - needed more force, and the cut just wasn't quite as perfect. So here's some pro's and con's for those of you that like such things:

PROS:
- They really do catch most clippings. Very nice
- All metal construction, no cheap crappy plastic like simlar Chinese mess-free clippers
- Extremely solid and NO CENTER POST! Seriously, I'm sick of snapping those. The lever in these has two pegs that mount firmly into holes on either side of the metal housing. I'm virtually certain that I will NEVER break these, even if I go rogue and use them on my toenails.
- Sharp. As. Hell. These are seriously the sharpest trimmers I've ever owned, and either they're sharpening them with robots, of they've got some prideful people doing a good job with it. Either way, good work guys! They cut very easily and leave a nice clean edge behind.
- Made in USA -- and while I'd call these crap if they were crap, I'm glad to say that I can be proud that these were made in my country
- Rock solid alignment of the cutting edges - some of the cheap trimmers I've had leave something to be desired here, but these are perfect.

Cons
- No file ... not really sure if this is a con, because if they keep cutting like they do now, I wont need it
- Can't put them in 'storage mode' (lever down) while the nail collection bin is closed. Basically you just have to find someplace to dump your trimmings before you can stow the lever.

All in all, I love these, and even though at $12 they're the highest priced trimmer I've ever bought, they are also unquestionably the best. I personally feel that they are worth the cost, to the point that immediately after trying out my first pair I ordered as second (to replace the garbage 'Trim' brand clippers that I keep in my car).

UPDATE: I have now had these and the Tweezermans for just over 4 months. The Tweezerman clippers are already starting to dull out & destroy my nails, but the No-Mes clippers are still great. I use both pairs about the same amount, however, I'm going to retire the Tweezermans - yes, Tweezerman will sharpen them for free, but the No-Mes clippers are still sharp without the hassle of having to get them sharpened. Whether or not you need the nail capturing feature, the real reason to buy these is that the cutting edge is bulletproof. Better than any clippers I've ever owned.

UPDATE (after about a year and a half): I've now lost both pairs of No-Mes clippers that I originally bought, but they easily made it through a year without getting bad. I'm now going to buy pair #3. I have seen several reviews that give these a low rating because they're too narrow for toes -- this is true, if you're looking fore a toe-nail clipper these are probably not for you. The width of the cutting head is restricted by the sides that have been added to help capture clippings. Since I don't use them for my toes, I still love these and strongly recommend them.
top_profane.title: The best clippers I've ever owned

top_rant.text:
Although Kristen Ess's Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo is advertised as being safe for anyone looking for a shampoo to remove build-up from products, please keep in mind this is a very drying formula and my scalp takes shampoos, conditioners,. treatments, and products with fragrances very easily! I even have used some silicone based products so this claims to remove the build-up! I also have a showerhead filter that I adore which I bought from Amazon & always wrap hair gently in a microfiber towel or a 100% cotton t-shirt! (Never rub ends or get abrasive! I have used the same leave in conditioning product & heat protector for a while but now can't even use any heat and had 2.buy scalp treatment for bald spots which I JUST grew back in!!!! My hair was so healthy I was planning on coloring it with some baby highlights and baylage foils using only the best bonding lighteners....but now just looking for hats, a Dr/Dermatologist, found a treatment but have time stamped hair and skin for a business channel and nothing changed but THIS (only used barely a dime sized amount as u can see from the bottle!).
Does THIS mean the same thing will occur to another person's hair? No!! Does it mean Kristen Ess's Hair line products are all the same? No! I buy products from brands often that have broken me out, for example, yet will buy different products from the exact same brand and they're staples in my routine!!!
So long as your hair is in good condition and you hydrate it well, & need to remove a ton of build-up for sure knowing that is the issue, have supplements, see a stylist, use lasers, have oily scalps im general then Kristen Ess's Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo could be lovely for You! I happen to know several content creators who use this shampoo and they absolutely LOVE it & have gorgeous hair!!!
Please ONLY use this if your scalp is oily, in great health bc there's something in THIS particular formula that made my hair dry and all clarifying shampoos can cause some dryness which is why it's important to use a conditioner and a mask after! That being said, I just had a bad reaction so do not let 1 reaction scare u away but think 🤔 about how a drying shampoo can bring out/ exacerbate any underlying scalp issues causing more dryness leading to possible breakage, hair loss. AGAIN, THIS does not mean it will happen to U! 💯 everyone has a different scalp & hair type!!! Kristen Ess's Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo did smell very elevated and I cannot stress enough that I have family members who will use the exact same products as me & we both have different effects!!!! This happens with haircare, skincare, food, books, memories, everything! I ONLY speak the Truth!
(Took pics daily 4 a business channel and noticed after I used this product clumps of hair came out and although 1 treatment soothes I am seeing a Dr in person bc the Dr who noticed it via a virtual visit even agreed 💯 with me that Everyone's hair type is different!
I recommend Kristen Ess's Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo to oily scalps!!!
When my hair was healthier, my scalp accumulated a lot of buildup as I used products w/ silicones, serums, creams, & more which led to them eventually weighing my hair down and making scalp/hair feel uncomfortable for so long! Upon initíal use ignoring ALL the hair coming out, Kristen Ess's Deep Clarifying Shampoo of merely a dime sized amt...it made my fine hair feel light weight, last 3 days w/o a shampoo and no greasiness while smelling delicious, voluminous, & gave hair that oomph I have not seen in a clarifying shampoo! But then, bald areas on top of scalp began and clumps came just moving hair literally. Although hair always has been fine, I always have had A LOT of it and never have seen hair come out so fast. When rinsing hair I'm holding onto (a little rat tail)..(lol 😂 can't stress/ makes hair worse!)...hair dries within minutes when just weeks ago before this, it took my hair up to 20 hours to fully air dry!
I have used clarifying shampoos before but it's so important to ensure your minerals and vitamins are all healthy, you're well hydrated, and if you JUST grew back bald areas patience is key as Kristen Ess's line has lots of other options! (Just bc I didn't assess my own hair/scalp issues.... I'm giving this 1 star ⭐ for ME but I STILL want to leave others knowing Kristen Ess's Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo could be a useful Shampoo 2 alternate but honestly make sure you have oily hair & VERY Healthy hair plus perhaps suggest a patch test on one small area especially if you have a ton of products and the build-up is causing excess sebum/product build -up which leaves a flat 'helmet head'/'hat head' look! Z
🙏If your scalp is dry, this just is not for U! I have to be honest! There are other Kristen Ess products out there that seem incredible!!
I always suggest using a filtered shower head from Amazon I adore, massage the scalp only lightly (never use nails), use a microfiber towel or a 100% cotton T-shirt to dry your hair!
It's crucial 2 know what your hair needs are and Kristen Ess offers a wide range of products! Once I get hair/scalp healthy again & hopefully grow hair back, I may look into Kristen Ess's gentle line!
See? I'm not scared away from Kristen Ess's Hair Care Products STILL so PLEASE Don't let 1 reaction deter u from buying a high end clarifying shampoo but know that I highly feel this is for someone w/ oily scalp, takes drying shampoos easily, has access to hydrating products,.a conditioner & mask,.leave in conditioner and extremely no sensitivities to shampoo!
If you have healthy hair/scalp, get regular salon visits (even 2 clean up the split ends with a slight trim every 6 weeks), use products for your hair type such as a leave in conditioner & a heat protector if you use heat styling &/ or silicones/oils, powders, dry shampoo, wax, hair pomade, hair gel, you name it, more conditioner for your hair than shampoo mid shaft down only, a pre-trearment, a bonding treatment, and a mask in shower....and want a clarifying shampoo...THIS is ur product!
**Remember a little goes a long way (depends on how thick/how much hair u have but start w/ a dime sized amt) as u can always add more & wish someone told me THIS upon 1st use! Also, Only rub this into the scalp and not the mid hair shaft or ends as the product does the work for you!! How innovative is THAT???
I appreciate that the fragrance is sophisticated and not overwhelming like many shampoos I've tried recently that work yet have been so harsh on my scalp bc of so much gaudy fragrance I've thought 🤔 "may I please have shampoo w/this perfume?" With Kristen Ess's Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo, I love the scent and after 3 days hair still smells elegant but not overwhelming...just fresh as if I just had my hair professionally cleaned at a salon! (Feels like that too!)
Definitely don't think this is a daily shampoo unless you have extreme buildup, severe excess sebum production, or wear a lot of wax over scalp DAILY but everyone is different! This is probably meant to be used weekly integrating this Kristen Ess Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo with a hydrating shampoo such as something like Kristen Ess's Hydrating shampoo or one of many of Kristen Ess's products for your hair type would work best or use this and go a few days between shampooing. Or there are some that appear more gentle which right now, I truly recommend assessing your hair/scalp's needs as this is a fabulous clarifying shampoo! My hair just needed some more help and a REALLY gentle approach as I had recently grown back bald areas! For most people, a clarifying shampoo especially if you don't live in harsh conditions, or have at least any humidity....THAT also makes a difference!!! So a leave in conditioner helps so MUCH!!!
Kristen Ess's Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo is cruelty-free, vegan, free of silicones, parabens, & phthalates. Kristen Ess's Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo uses "zip-up" technology which is designed to go into weak areas of hair, sealing the cuticle, mending split ends, protecting hair from environmental stressors/color fade.
I noticed my hair felt/looked lighter but then it was so dry pieces came out which just is bc MY hair type & condition/ state currently needs Kristen Ess's Sulfate-free free shampoo instead! ⁸Please know that normally THIS would work on my hair but assess where your hair state is in realistically and at LEAST try it out if you fall under the guidelines I mentioned for a clarifying shampoo!
I still want to use Kristen Ess's other products so THAT should tell you how lovely it is and no I don't buy anything just for the scent unless it's perfume! (The light, elegant scent just is a happy bonus!
I definitely want to try more products from Kristen Ess but will make sure they're catered 2 My particular needs which at THIS time means no sulphates and definitely some of Kristen Ess's conditioners as my scalp is really not healthy enough for a clarifying shampoo right NOW. I suggest asking your dermatologist and/or definitely choosing Kristen Ess's Hydrating conditioners, treatments, and especially Kristen Ess's Sulfate-free shampoo as I imagine it's a more gentle formula but if you have hair that gets oily and have a solid regimen...get THIS Deep Clean Clarifying Shampoo!!! Recommend Usage is 1x weekly & adjust from there but all depends on your hair!
If your hair is healthy, in need of a good cleaning, although directions say to follow with a conditioner "or" a hair mask, I feel both are needed w/ this product! Even just a little conditioner on the ends and a mask spread evenly through hair.. what works 4 U is key! Regular salon visits even if it's microscopic trims, a good leave in conditioner, follow w/ a mask and a real bonding treatment! Then use a leave in conditioner and a heat protection product always! It is important to protect scalp from sun UVA/UVB rays too! Use products for Ur hair type!
I definitely want to try more Kristen Ess products as now I've tasted the Kool-Aid and don't want to drink water 😜 (metaphorically speaking)! Just bc THIS may not work right NOW & is drying, keep in mind it's bc my hair has some scalp issues that aren't JUST build-up so be sure that's 100% what you have!!! Remember that a clarifying shampoo is only meant for use 1X weekly up to 3X for some???? WOW! So it's to alternate w/ ur regular shampoo which could cause build-up!? Alternate regardless as this can be used with another shampoo like Kristen Ess's Sulfate-free shampoo if you're unsure! Less is more with this too and nutrients for hair are vital
Ask Dr 4 supps as I can't tell U what 2 get! Good
Foods 4 hair: eggs, chicken, legumes, soy, berries, spinach, and more but ask YOUR Dietician/Nutritionist please about diet/supps 4 U, see a Dermatologist, and definitely ask a hair stylist professional not trying to sell you their products but read ingredients as they mostly don't lie and I have genuine excitement for trying Kristen Ess's conditioners, & definitely the sulfate-free shampoo and MORE!!!) I love the scent so I pray 🙏 the fragrance was not the catalyst! I have used MUCH stronger scented shampoos when Kristen Ess's Shampoo's scent has such a delicate, eloquent balance! I went bald bald and Amazon won't help at ALL! They won't help with a refund? Nothing not on any other products after 30++ hours of calls promising, no knocks, other items that have given me chemical burns not from haircare, and 4.6hrs on chat and IF I could go 2 a drop site I would!! But have each name and they refuse but will promise & nothing is done! I'll retract when they help at ALL, or I'll.keep going!
Hope this helps!
