top_rant.text:
This is my first time to ever write a review on a makeup product (even though I am a makeup junkie who has tried pretty much every product known to man over the years) so that alone should tell you just how impressed I am by this primer! Let me start by prefacing this with the fact I have extremely dry skin. It's been difficult to get used to considering the fact I had oily to oily/combination skin my entire life until I gave birth to baby #3 (2.5 years ago)! So now (at 34 years old) I have had to relearn my skin and find new products that work with dry skin! Along with all the changes to my skin I've also noticed my pores have, unfortunately, become even larger (if that's possible lol) and considerably more noticeable! If I want my makeup to go on even somewhat smoothly and to not look caked on or patchy I have to complete my normal skin care routine (making sure my face is as moisturized as possible) followed by applying a great primer! As far as primers go I feel like I've tried literally everything!

★Here's a list of a few primers I tried that I didn't have great results with:
-Smashbox Photo Finish
-NYX Pore Filler
-Benefit POREfessional Pore Minimizing
(original & hydrating)
-Urban Decay All Nighter
-Tarte Face Tape Primer
-Revlon Photo Ready
-Wet n Wild Prime Focus
-Maybeline Baby Skin Instant Pore Eraser
-Clinique Pore Refining Primer

★ A list of primers I had fairly decent results with:
-Neutrogena Hydro Boost Glow Primer + Serum (✫I actually use this right before the DHC Velvet primer a lot of the time now & they work beautifully together!✫)
-Tula Filter Priming & Blurring
-Lancôme La Base Pro (Oil Free).
-Elf Cosmetics Poreless Putty
(my skin is just too dry for it)

I had just about gotten to the point of accepting that my days of having that perfect, flawless look with my makeup were behind me when I happened upon a TikTok! I had never seen this creator before but instantly fell in love with her candor and genuine personality. As silly as it may sound, I am incredibly thankful I found her page and that she was gracious enough to show her untouched, unfiltered skin up close so that the viewers could see that she too had dry skin AND large, noticeable pores (those of you who love how makeup can make you feel more like yourself and more...idk...alive...probably get it). After expressing how important your skin care routine was if you wanted to achieve a similar look and have your foundation go on smoothly she began to talk about her new favorite primer! I started to roll my eyes as she went on and on about how remarkable this primer was because I had heard it a thousand times before with different products (especially those that are sponsored) but I was surprised to see that this user was actually not sponsored and something about her just seemed genuine. Ordinarily, the person doing their makeup is pretty young and has perfectly smooth, glowing skin going into the tutorial so it doesn't really matter what they use because it's all going to look pretty smooth 🤷‍♀️! I was definitely looking forward to seeing the results of this primer she was raving about since, like I said previously, she had allowed us to see she had slightly matured, textured skin with larger pores. After applying her moisturizers and serums she gave them a minute to absorb and then began to apply the DHC Velvet Skin Coat. I double checked to make sure there were not any beauty filters on because her skin instantly looked incredible and so, so smooth! Could a primer alone really do that?! She went on to apply both her concealer and foundation and I was sitting there in complete and total awe. She had not even set her makeup and already she looked like she had a filter on because her skin was smooth and pretty! Once she applied her setting powder and completed her look I was 150% sold! I went to the comments and saw that several other women (in their early 20's through their 40's) were raving about their positive experiences with the primer as well! I quickly opened my Ulta app and typed in "DHC Velvet Skin Coat" and there it was! I was pleasantly surprised by the price (I was expecting something crazy expensive considering the results it had given) and couldn't get it ordered quickly enough! A couple of days later and I had my new "TikTok made me buy it" primer in my hands! I prepped by face by using my normal go-to creams and serums and after giving it a little bit to absorb I sat down to try my new primer! As hopeful and excited as I was I also knew from experience how likely it was that this primer would be no different than the other 20 primers I had tried. I applied approximately an eraser-sized amount of the primer onto the back of my hand and then began to evenly distribute the product all over my face (I made sure to pay special attention to the large pores around my nose). I looked up into the mirror and was absolutely speechless! My somewhat red, somewhat splotchy, somewhat dry, somewhat uneven skin looked almost flawless! And my pores?! What pores?! What kind of magic was this?! I couldn't believe what I was seeing. I even got up and walked across the house to show my husband (who could not possibly know less about makeup if he tried!)! All I said was "how does my makeup look so far?" Reminder: I had on zero makeup! 😬 He looked up at me, smiled and said "Wow! You look beautiful! How did you get ready so fast?!" He thought I was done y'all! I mean given the fact I ordinarily only apply a little bit of concealer, foundation, powder and blush to run a quick errand or two (we have three young sons so if ya know, ya know!) and he wasn't looking for me to have on mascara, eyebrows, lip color, etc. I was pretty pumped that he actually thought I had done my makeup!! I wish I could adequately describe what it is this primer does but just know that it reduces the appearance of your pores and any fine lines and it has the most velvety look and feel to it, almost creating a sort of blurring effect (if that makes sense) while completely smoothing out your complexion. If this was how good my skin looked with primer alone I couldn't wait to see what it looked like once I applied my makeup on top of it! Ya'll, I about fell out of my chair when I saw how beautiful my concealer and foundation went on! I figured the real test would be how it looked when I applied my setting powder because so many times this is when my skin would end up looking dry, flaky and more textured. Let me tell you, this little DHC Velvet Skin Coat is MAGIC in a bottle! Legit magic! ✨ I applied the tiniest bit of powder I could get away with using (like I always do) and was just totally in awe of how flawless my skin and makeup looked! Isn't it the absolute best feeling when something you're excited about actually lives up to the hype?! This primer has become my go-to and after using it for a little over a month I've found a few tricks that help it live up to its full potential for my skin! If you have dry skin and/or are in your 30's I recommend applying a tiny bit of the Neutrogena Hydro Boost Glow Primer + Serum first (after you've already moisturized and prepped your skin, of course 😉) followed by applying maybe the size of a pencil eraser of the DHC Velvet Skin Coat Primer. A little goes a long way! Trust me! I always let my primer sit for a minute (literally like a minute lol) and then I apply my concealer followed by my foundation. Also, something pretty important to consider when picking out your primer, foundation and concealer is to make sure they all have the same base. For example, if you buy a silicone based primer you (in theory) don't want to buy a water based foundation. Mixing a silicone based primer with a water based foundation (or an oil-based primer with a silicone based foundation) isn't a guaranteed disaster or anything, I have just found that by taking the advice to use products with the same base I have had much better results with my makeup! Additionally, I'll add a couple of good silicone based foundations and concealers I've tried for those of you new to all of this! ☺️ These are a couple I've used and had good results with on my dry skin:
1) Estée Lauder Double Wear Foundation (around $45 but lasts you forever and is for sure worth the money!) and
2) Maybelline New York Fit Me Dewy + Smooth Foundation (this one costs less than $10 and has a myriad of positive reviews)! For concealer:
1)Tarte Shape Tape Concealer ( Costs abt $30 but is worth it for sure!)
2)NYX PROFESSIONAL MAKEUP HD Studio Photogenic (Amazing quality for less than $5!!)
This is, of course, all just my opinions based on personal experience and an obsessive amount of research! I do hope it helps some of you and maybe even saves you some money!
So, in conclusion, YES!! this primer is genuinely THAT good! So good, in fact, it inspired me to write a literal novel for a review! Haha! I have just seriously never been more impressed with a product y'all! I know this review is super long but I thought if it was able to help even one person out there who felt lost in this world of endless products (and all the paid to leave positive reviews kind of reviews we've seen on other not-so-great products 😒) then it was worth it! I also know how overwhelming it can be to try and figure out if a product is right for you when you're ordering online so I wanted to give as much information as possible! Buy this primer! You won't regret it!!
top_rant.title: Best Primer On The Market!

top_rant.text:
This comes in an outer product box. It has bubble wrap protecting it. When you remove the bubble wrap, the palette is sealed with plastic around it. The outer product box looks just like the palette front and back. The palette is made in China. The palette is from the company Komar Cosmetics. It says it is cruelty free and does have the newest PETA logo and says “PETA Cruelty-Free and Vegan.” However, I went to the PETA website where you can search for PETA approved companies. I did a search for Komar Cosmetics and it did NOT come up. I saw on the box it says, “Manufacturer: Zhejiang Komar Cosmetics Co., Ltd.” so I also looked up that, and it did NOT come up. So, it doesn’t seem to actually be PETA approved. Also, looking at the logo, it looks a little off. The pink is much darker than the PETA approved logo, and the writing is not the same either. You can do a search for “See the New Beauty Without Bunnies Logo” to see the difference. On the PETA website you can also search for PETA approved companies.

The ingredients are listed on the back. Along with it saying it’s cruelty free, it also says it is vegan, contains no talc, no mineral oil, no parabens, no phthalates, and no gluten. Given that the PETA logo seems to be false, I’m not sure how much I believe of that. Of course, given the ingredients listed, some can be verified, if those are the real ingredients. It does contain mica. It also contains bamboo stem powder and chia seed oil, and mimosa bark and mangosteen peel extracts.

The palette is cardboard. It has a nice design. It has a mirror inside on the lid. It isn’t the full size of the lid, but it is a good size. There was a protective plastic sheet over the shades. The pans are small, but I always expect that with palettes with this many shades in it. They also aren’t filled completely to the top, there is some space. Some seem more filled than others. I’m not sure if they really aren’t filled to the top or if they just aren’t as lifted and close to the top as others. There is a smell to the palette if you put your nose right up in it. It’s the typical smell with cheaper palettes from Amazon that I’ve gotten used to. It always is gone after I’ve had the palette open for a short time.

For the swatches, I did finger swatches on my clean forearm (no primer, lotion, etc.). In the photos I labeled how many swipes (layers) I did for each.

There’s 48 shades total - 17 mattes, 15 of what I’d call shimmers, 12 of what I’d call metallics, 2 of what I’d call satins, and 2 pressed glitters.

Most of the mattes swatched well and performed really well. They were soft, smooth, and most had great pigment. They were easy to blend too.

The 12 shades that I’d call metallics were GORGEOUS! They were soft, easy to pick up, applied great, were pigmented, and had beautiful shine.

The 15 shades that I’d call shimmers were HORRIBLE! In the swatches I had to build them up with 2-3 layers, sometimes as many as 5! They felt like that were hard to pick up, but I’d look at my finger and they looked beautiful. But, when I went to swatch them there was just hardly anything. On the eyes they performed the same. Just no pigment and blah.

There were 2 shades that I’d call satins. They performed well. They were soft, smooth, and pigmented.

I didn’t try either of the 2 pressed glitters. I just avoid pressed glitters. I have a love/hate relationship with glitter. Love how pretty it looks on, but hate how it gets EVERYWHERE. So, I avoid them!

I did a breakdown of each individual shade at the end of the review if you want more details.

It’s funny because with cheaper palettes if there’s shades that don’t perform well, in my experience it’s usually mattes - dry, patchy, not pigmented, hard to blend - and shimmers are usually great. But NOT with this palette. With this palette, MOST of the mattes are GREAT, and the shimmers are HORRIBLE! Usually with shimmers, they’ll pick up good with a finger, but sometimes not pick up well with a brush. These wouldn’t even pick up well with my finger! That’s crazy! They were pretty disappointing.

Overall, the palette is okay. The mattes are great for the most part. The metallics are GORGEOUS! Honestly, it’s great theres 17 mattes and 12 metallics, because they save this palette! The mattes alone wouldn’t make getting the palette worth it. The mattes and the metallics make it so the palette isn’t a complete waste. If you’re looking for colors that this palette has in mattes and the metallics, I’d say get it. However, if the shades that are catching your eye are the shimmers, I’d say skip it! There’s so many other palettes available on Amazon, many that are at the same price point that perform so much better, and where all the shades in the palette perform great. So, I’d only recommend this palette if you are okay with 34 (or 36 if you like pressed glitters) of the 48 shades, and if the shades that have caught your eye and made you look at this palette in the first place aren’t the shimmers.

BREAKDOWN OF INDIVIDUAL SHADES BY ROW:
Row 1
6 mattes, 1 metallic, 1 shimmer
The first 6 are mattes. They were all soft, smooth, and pigmented. For the swatches, I did 1 swipe for each.
The 7th shade in the row is a metallic. It is soft, almost creamy feeling, kind of chunky when you pick it up with your finger, but smooths out beautifully. Very pigmented. Swatch is 1 swipe.
The 8th shade is a shimmer. Smooth, not chunky at all. Picks up easily with finger. Pigmented. Nice sheen to it. Swatch is 1 swipe.

Row 2
7 shimmers, 1 matte.
The first 3 are shimmers. They feel kind of dry. They picked up okay on the finger, but not great. I had to do 3 swipes for each to get the swatches you see.
The 4th is a shimmer. It is soft, almost creamy feeling. Great pigment. Only 1 swipe!
The 5th, 6th, and 7th are just like the first 3. I did 3 swipes for each. The 7th was definitely the worst. It came off so sheer. Even the third layer it still was pretty sheer.
The 8th is a matte. It’s soft, smooth, and good pigment. I did 2 swipes for the swatch.

Row 3
5 mattes, 2 shimmers, 1 metallic.
First 5 are mattes. They were all soft, smooth, and pigmented. The first one I did 2 swipes for, the rest only 1 swipe.
The 6th is a shimmer. It was dry and hard to pick up like the others I described. So disappointing because it could be a beautiful shade. It took 5 swipes to get the swatch you see. I was hoping it would work better on the eyes, but didn’t have much hope.
The 7th is a shimmer. It felt softer and creamier, but was disappointing too. It took 3 swipes for the swatch.
The 8th is finally another metallic! Soft, almost creamy feeling, smooth, pigmented. Picks up easily on the finger, can be a little chunky but not much, and smoothes out completely. The swatch is 1 swipe.

Row 4
3 metallics, 4 mattes, 1 pressed glitter.
First 2 are metallics. Soft, almost creamy feeling. Easy to pick up with finger, a little chunky but smooth out beautifully. Very pigmented. The swatches are each 1 swipe.
The 3rd, 4th, 5th, and 6th are mattes. They are all soft, smooth, and pigmented. They are each 1 swipe.
The 7th is a metallic. Soft, almost creamy feeling. Easy to pick up with finger, a little chunky but smooth out beautifully. Very pigmented. Definitely makes up for the crap pink in the 3rd row 😂 The swatch is 1 swipe.
The 8th is a pressed glitter. I just avoid those. I have a love/hate relationship with glitter. Love how pretty it looks on, but hate how it gets EVERYWHERE. So, I avoid pressed glitters!

Row 5
5 metallics, 3 shimmers
The first 5 are thankfully metallics! Soft, almost creamy feeling. Easy to pick up with finger, a little chunky but smooth out beautifully. Very pigmented. They are each 1 swipe.
The 6th is a shimmer. It’s one of those dry, hard to pick up ones. Swatch is 3 swipes.
The 7th is a shimmer. It felt a little softer, picked up a little easier. The swatch is 2 swipes.
The 8th is a shimmer. Another one of the dry, hard to pick up ones. Swatch is 3 swipes.

Row 6
1 matte, 1 pressed glitter, 2 metallics, 2 shimmers, 2 satins.
The 1st is a matte. It is soft, smooth, and pigmented. Swatch is 1 swipe.
The 2nd is a pressed glitter.
The 3rd is a metallic. It had a marbled appearance. It is the MOST BEAUTIFUL of the whole palette! I was really hoping this wasn’t going to be one of the crappy shimmers, so I was really happy it wasn’t! It’s soft, creamy feeling. Picks up so easy with a finger, glides onto the skin easily, and has such a wet, metallic look! Very pigmented. It’s beautiful! The swatch is 1 swipe.
The 4th is a shimmer. It isn’t as dry as the others. I was able to pick it up a little easier. The swatch is 2 swipes.
The 5th is a satin. It is soft, picks up easily with the finger, pigmented. The swatch is 1 swipe.
The 6th is a metallic. Soft, almost creamy feeling. Picks up easily with the finger. Pigmented. The swatch is 1 swipe.
The 7th is a shimmer. One of the dry, hard to pick up ones, this one was another really disappointing one. It would be so beautiful! The swatch is 3 swipes.
The 8th surprised me. It’s black so I was expecting a matte, but it seems more like a satin. It’s soft, creamy feeling, picks up very easily with the finger. The swatch is 1 swipe.
top_rant.title: 34 Shades worth it, 15 shimmers HORRIBLE, & unsure if it’s really cruelty free

top_rant.text:
Morpho's lip stains are AMAZING; the ABSOLUTE BEST I have ever used! I seriously cannot rave enough about both Zinnia and Snap Dragon! (And Morpho's other products, lip glosses and lip pencils, are amazing too). EVERYTHING about these stains is FANTASTIC! And believe me, I've tried every lip stain "known to mankind"!!! I have tried everything from the cheap, drugstore brands to the more expensive, upscale/luxury lines.

I am very ill, with a debilitating combination of autoimmune diseases. I've been homebound and bedridden for years. Prior to getting sick, I never wore much makeup, except for lipstick. I've always loved wearing lip color. Even just a hint of color on the lips can really brighten up one's face and make a huge difference (especially when a chronic illness often times causes one to appear pale and sickly). Once I became bedridden, traditional lipstick was no longer an option. If I fall asleep in bed (or even if I'm awake, but just want to roll over and rest my head on the pillow), I don't want lipstick smeared all over my pillowcases and sheets (nor do I want it smeared all over my face). Lip stains became my answer and I won't ever go back to wearing lipstick again.The good stains (such as Morpho's) last all day and you never need to worry about the color transferring onto things (your teeth, cups/glasses, pillowcases, etc.) Lip stains are the way to go! And Morpho's Zinnia and Snap Dragon are the BEST! You can go all day without having to reapply or worrying about smudges. Just apply in the morning and that's it! You're set until it's time to go to bed!

For the past several years, I've been wearing Vincent Longo's "Liquid Kiss" and "Neo Aura", mixed together. I've also been wearing Lorac's "Sheer Magic". I found these to be the best lip stains on the market, in regards to both color and staying power. I've been wearing these for years, so you can imagine how I totally "freaked out" when it came time to place my usual order for the 3 above-mentioned stains and discovered that beauty.com no longer carries any of them! I then checked Sephora's website, but both Lorac's and VL's stains are listed as being "No longer available." So I called Sephora to see if I could find out any more info and I was told by the rep that Sephora used to carry both Lorac's and VL's stains, but that they no longer carry either (in any shade). The rep I spoke with said that both are "Permanently out of stock." So, I finally got on Amazon, and discovered Morpho lip stains. And I'm so THANKFUL that I did!

Where do I begin... ZINNIA HAS NOW BECOME MY FAVORITE LIP STAIN, EVER!!! First, the color is PERFECT; gorgeous and sophisticated! It is a beautiful berry-brown. The color is very similar to the shade I used to create when I'd combine VL's Liquid Kiss with Neo Aura, but MUCH BETTER! And I no longer have to hassle with combining two colors together anymore. I can't imagine a skin tone which Zinnia wouldn't look great on. And by the way, my fiancé thinks Zinnia looks great on me (when we kiss, he appreciates the fact that the color doesn't transfer off, onto him).

It is extremely easy to apply. Using the wand with the sponge tip applicator, I apply Zinnia to my lower lip and then I rub my lower and upper lips together... and Voila! Perfection in just a matter of seconds! I love that Zinnia is so versatile! You can achieve many looks with just this one color, depending on how much you use. For a light, very natural look, use very little. For a deep, rich, vibrant look, use more. Play around with it to get just the look you want. And if you happen to apply too much and it looks too dark, it's easy to correct. Just put a tiny bit of water on a paper towel and evenly blot your lips with it, until the color lightens up to the shade you prefer.

When I first tried Zinnia, it felt so moisturizing on my lips, I thought to myself, "Oh no! This isn't going to have good staying power!" Boy, was I wrong! It is extremely long-lasting, from morning to night. Even after eating three meals and snacks, and drinking beverages throughout the day, the color stays put. Not sure if this makes a difference, but I do use an olive-oil/beeswax based chapstick over it. Maybe that helps to seal in the color? I have Sjogren's (an autoimmune disease that causes severe dryness - for me, it really affects my eyes and lips) and I have no problems at all with Morpho's lip stains. They DO NOT dry my lips out. In fact, they are the least drying lip stains I've ever used (Except for Stila's. However, I have issues with Stila's stains - pigment is a problem and they're not very long lasting).

Also, Zinnia is completely weightless. Feels as if you're wearing nothing! And if you're at all concerned about it having too strong of a peppermint flavor, don't be! I was a bit concerned too as I don't care for strong flavors. I can taste only just a hint of peppermint when I first apply and then after maybe a minute, I don't notice any flavor whatsoever. That's a huge plus for me!

I'm so THRILLED to have discovered Morpho's lip stains! If you've been searching for a lip stain with the following formula/characteristics: super easy to apply, weightless, non-drying, long-lasting, no strange taste, versatile/gorgeous color (can appear both light/natural or dark/bold or somewhere in the middle, depending on the amount you use), then Zinnia is for you!!! I would HIGHLY RECOMMEND this to anyone and everyone! I am ECSTATIC that I discovered such an amazing product! (If you happen to be bedridden, for whatever reason, this DOES NOT come off. I have applied these lip stains, fallen asleep on my cream-colored pillowcases, and have never once seen a stain!)

I should add that every Morpho product I've tried, has been awesome! "Snap Dragon" is the other lip stain made by Morpho. I was leery at first, because most reds do not look good on me. Snap Dragon is described as "the best red ever" and it truly is. When I first applied it, it was very intense; I would never wear it that bold during the day. Unfortunately, due to my chronic illness, I'm unable to go out for drinks, dinner, dancing, etc., but if I could, Snap Dragon would be the perfect red to wear (and I'd wear it bold/vibrant). However, since I'm just at home, I tone down the color. I just dampen a paper towel with a bit of water and evenly blot my lips to tone down the intensity. Again, Voila! The perfect color! You really can't go wrong with either Zinnia or Snap Dragon; just play around with them until you find the right intensity of color that looks great on you.

Morpho also makes Super Staying Lip Glosses. I've tried "Nabi" and I really like it! It's a beautiful nude brown. Very shiny. Very sheer. Very moisturizing. Very natural looking (especially when worn alone). It's beautiful when applied over either Zinnia or Snap Dragon. "Cho Cho" is also very pretty! Everything I said about Nabi applies to Cho Cho, except Cho Cho is definitely darker (more intense); a very pretty reddish-brown. Neither are too sticky feeling like many other glosses out there on the market. To be honest, I probably won't be wearing these glosses at home, only because I'm bedridden and as I mentioned above, I don't want to stain my pillowcases and sheets. But I did go to a doctor's appointment recently, and I wore Zinnia with Nabi applied over it; what a wonderful, beautiful combo! Will definitely wear the glosses whenever I do leave the house.

I have also tried Morpho's Lip Pencil in "Anamu" - a very pretty, rosy-brown color, with just a subtle hint of gold to it (when I say "gold" do NOT think glitter. This is very sophisticated). Very creamy. Looks great worn under Nabi lip gloss! This combo gave my lips a natural, but very sexy, pouty, full look (my fiancé loved it!) I haven't tried it yet, but the lip pencils can be worn over the lip stains to give more of a creamy look, rather than matte. It's really up to you and your particular preference. They are all wonderful products, so I just say have fun and play around with different combinations. You really can't go wrong!

And I can't end this review without talking about the founder of Morpho, Janine Greff. When I called Morpho to ask some questions, prior to ordering, Janine answered the phone herself! Aside from being extremely knowledgeable about lip products and the makeup industry, in general (she is a top makeup artist), she was unbelievably warm and friendly! She spent a lot of time on the phone with me, answering all of my questions and helping me to decide on the right color choices for me. She is one of the most helpful, kind, down-to-earth, easy to talk to, generous women, I have ever had the pleasure of speaking with!!! I have never experienced such AMAZING customer service!My products arrived quickly and came beautifully packaged in the most elegant, black organza bag. Janine included a personally signed note, with tips and ideas for how to use the products. She really understands women and what they want! It is obvious, just how much she wants to please her customers. She REALLY pleased me!

From now on, I will only be purchasing Morpho products for my lips! I have finally found the perfect products and the perfect customer service to match! I highly recommend Morpho's line of lip products to anyone! I'm sure you will be very pleased! And after speaking with Janine, it's obvious that she wants her customers to be happy and very satisfied with their purchase! If you receive a color that just isn't right for you, she's the type of person that will gladly let you exchange it for something else. Awesome customer service!!!

Michelle G.
top_rant.title: Zinnia is AMAZING! Absolute BEST lip stain ever!

top_rant.text:
This comes in a nice kind of peachy outer product box. It’s simple with just the brand name, what I’d call a slogan, and Instagram handle. It doesn’t say where it’s made or anything like that.

Inside, the brushes are in a plastic sleeve that is sealed. I liked that. You had to cut it open. There was also another plastic sleeve that was sealed that has a little plastic brush bag inside. Inside the sleeve with the brushes, the brushes were each in thick plastic sleeves.

The brush handles are a peach color. The brand name is on one side and the brush number and label is on the other (for example, “212 Blending Eyeshadow Large”).”

I previously got a single brush from this brand (“226 Detail Eyeshadow” brush) and when I first took it out of the packaging and ran my fingers across the top of the brush, there was a bunch of dust and small particles coming off of it. I’d never had anything like that happen before, even with cheap sets I’ve gotten. So, I was hoping that wouldn’t occur with any of the brushes in this set! Thankfully, it didn’t! They all appeared fine with no dust or particles coming off of the hairs!

The hairs are all very soft.

The bag is okay. It’s just a thin plastic material. It’s really cheap looking, not what I expected with a set that costs this much. It doesn’t even have any kind of snap closure. Instead it has a little metal nub and there’s a small hole in the plastic, you put the hole onto the metal nub. It’s so odd! Again, I’ve gotten cheap sets that came with brush bags that were a heck of a lot nicer than this! With a set of 5 brushes that cost a whopping $95 (at the time of writing this review), I would expect it to come with a much better brush bag! Heck, I honestly would have been okay without a brush bag at all. But, including one that is just this thin, cheap plastic thing is worse to me. That could just be me though!

There’s 5 brushes
“212 Blending Eyeshadow Large” - This is natural hairs. Looking up the individual brush on the brands website I found that this is made of wool hairs. For a blending brush that is considered “large” this is on the smaller side, which I LOVE! I have really small, really hooded eyes. So, usually regular sized blending brushes are way too big for me, let alone large ones. But, this one is a perfect blending brush for me! It’s pinched so it’s flat on each side. It’s perfect to apply shadow in the crease and blend it out. It’s also perfect to blend out any harsh lines I have. It’s the perfect size medium to large blending brush for my really small, really hooded eyes. But, anyone who has eyes that large blending brushes usually work for, will probably be surprised how small this is compared to those. I took a photo comparing it to a blending brush I consider to be large to show. The brush is really soft. It’s natural haired. It picks up product well, applies it nicely, and blends it out well.

“219 Small Precision Smudge” - This is a flat brush that goes to a point. Its synthetic. It’s small. It works great to apply liner on the upper or lower lash line, apply inner corner or brow bone highlight, and it also works great to use to cut my crease.

“215 Finish Concealer” - This is a typical large flat concealer brush. It’s synthetic. It works well to apply concealer or any kind of shimmer/metallic shadow.

“203 Point Eyeshadow” - This is a smaller version of the 212 brush. The hairs are natural. Looking up the individual brush on the brands website I found that this is made of wool hairs. The hairs are shorter, so it’s denser. It can pack eyeshadow on, but it can also blend out harsh lines. When you want to blend out a harsh line, but only want to get the harsh area, not anywhere else, this brush works great for that. It’s also great to apply product right where you want it and blend it out. This brush is great for my really small, really hooded eyes.

“225 Small Crease” - This is a fluffy kind of pencil brush. It is natural haired. Looking up the individual brush on the brands website I found that this is made of weasel hairs. I absolutely love it! It’s perfect for my really small, really hooded eyes. It applies product right where I want it, but it can also blend product out. It works to apply shadow on the lower lash line and blend it out. It’s also great if you want to blend out a small harsh area, and only blend out that specific area. It’s a great brush to have!

I think this set is a great set for people like me with really small, really hooded eyes! It’s hard to find brushes that aren’t too big for your eyes. It’s hard to find blending brushes especially. This has 3 brushes that are perfect to do any blending you’d want to do! It’s a pricey set, but the brushes are nice quality and work really well.

I had my doubts about this brand after my first experience with the brand when I got a single brush of theirs (“226 Detail Eyeshadow” brush) and it seemed to be dirty with dust/dirt all in the hairs. But I liked the brush after I washed it. But, this set definitely redeemed the brand in my eyes. I still think the brand is pretty pricey. This set is a whopping $95! That seems crazy expensive for a set of 5 eye brushes! But, it breaks down to $19 a brush. I’m a makeup fanatic and love makeup brushes. I’ve tried tons of brushes from affordable to high end. I’ve had amazing affordable ones and crappy affordable ones, just like I’ve had amazing high end ones and crappy high end ones. My favorite natural haired brushes are Rephr brushes. Those are handmade, hand cut Japanese brushes. Ones like that are expensive. Rephr eye brushes are $24, so with this set having 5 brushes that break down to $19 a brush, the price isn’t so bad. However, if you go to the Rephr website, you can read all about how they make their brushes, what kinds of hair they use, etc. I’d never heard of “The Tool Lab” before, so I did a little research into the brand. I went to their website. There I looked at the “Brand Story” and it said that the founder of The Tool Lab “has presented high-quality products at reasonable price.” Personally, I don’t think $95 for a set of 5 eye brushes ($19 a brush) can be considered a “reasonable price.” For a natural haired, high quality eyeshadow brush, I’d say around $20-$25 is reasonable. The page with the “Brand Story” on it goes on and on about how they make high quality products and what a “toolist” is, but doesn’t say anything about how the brushes are made or what kind of hairs they use. If you go to the product page for this specific brush it tells you the same thing the Amazon product page does. I do know from the single brush of theirs that I got that the ferrules are aluminum and the handles are wood. For this brush set, it doesn’t actually state what type of hairs are used. It just says “signature bristles made by The Tool Lab effortlessly load shadow and deposit beautiful hues on your face.” Looking up each individual brush on their website does lead you to what type of hairs are used, so that’s nice. But, you shouldn’t have to go looking for the information. At this price point, it should be provided on the product page for the entire brush set.

Overall, I think this is a great set! If you have really small, really hooded eyes like me I definitely recommend this set! Even if you don’t have really small and/or really hooded eyes, if you’re looking for some great small blending or detail brushes, I recommend this set! They’re well made, work great, feel nice, and look nice. I do think they’re pretty price. Are they worth the high price tag? That’s for you to decide. Personally, for natural haired eye brushes I usually pay $24, so $19 per brush isn’t bad. But the natural haired brushes I spend $24 per brush on I know the are hand made in Japan, how they’re made, etc. These brushes I don’t know where they’re made or how they’re made (are the hairs uncut or cut, are they done by hand or machine, etc.). I love the sizes of the brushes so if I wanted to get multiples of these brushes and couldn’t find equivalent Rephr brushes, I would purchase these.
top_rant.title: Nice set, brushes perfect sizes for my really small, really hooded eyes!

top_rant.text:
Though I'm no licensed barber, I've been cutting hair (most of the time, my own) for more than 20 years. I have only the best in my arsenal; (1). Andis Master Clippers and (2). Andis T-Outliner (which replaced my Styliner's years ago). I have had Oster's and WAHL's in my arsenal in the past, but anyone that knows about clippers will tell you that Andis makes solid, reliable products that just do not break (unless you fail to oil them). That said, I needed something 'additional' to go with 1 and 2.

I don't want to make this review 'racially charged' (LLLOOOLLL!!!!! XD), but just for the heck of it, I'm a black male/person/human being with sensitive skin; I cannot use razors on my face, scalp, cheek, chin, neck... you name it, I absolutely cannot afford to use razors, or else the end result will be bump city. It's THE most irritating/itchy/painful experience EVER! I'm NOT going back! LOL!!! Anyway, enough of the flashbacks. Though I can't afford to use a razor, I still want to get (the shave/cut) close enough to where my head/scalp and neck/throat are 'bald' and smooth. Here was the problem with 1 and 2.

The Master Clippers (out-of-the-box) are not zero gap. It's fairly simple to get them that way, though. However, due to the nature of the Master Clippers, as in their being adjustable for fading and such, they may not stay that way for a long time (even with the On The Money blade setters). That means the larger lower blade and the smaller upper blade may end up overlapping or underlapping. If the tip of the upper blade is set above the tip of the lower blade (overlap), you're in for one bad experience. =O It'll be way too sharp!*** If it's the other way around (underlap), then you won't get expected results because the cut won't be low/fine enough. Those Master Clippers are the best clippers and when the blades are aligned appropriately, some folks can use them (solely) to get optimal results: cutting, fading, shaving, edging. I opted to give mine a very, very low (but-not-quite-zero) gap, so on my head/scalp, the cut isn't fine enough. On my neck/throat, the shave isn't fine enough (going against and then with the grain), but I still get irritation there afterwards... meaning when I apply rubbing alcohol after the shave, I get a bit of a sting! Sheesh.

The T-Outliners are great!!! It doesn't get any better than those. I have now used them for quite some time to cut my head/scalp 'bald' (I keep listing bald as 'bald' because to me, if you can still feel stubble with your hand going against the grain after your shave, then your head isn't as bald as it can get... it just looks bald xD) but I used them in the past mostly for edging. Now, the thing with this superior pair of clippers is this: I can't go against the grain on certain parts of my head/scalp because I'll get bumps. So I do it where I can and go with the grain where I can't. Also, these clippers have a tendency to get 'caught' in certain hair-thickened areas because they're not made for balding. After an initial shave, where I position the blades flat on my head/scalp (picture a shovel in the snow) then I have to run through my head/scalp again for a smoother/finishing shave (picture a pair of clippers when being used for edging... or a paint brush's tip at a zero-degree angle on the surface being painted). That's at least twice I have to run through my daggone head/scalp!!! I mean, it ends up being great at the end, but it takes a while. Now, the T-Outliners are forbidden from the neck/throat area. Why? Because they ARE zero gap out the box. And they let you know, son! Though the shave isn't as fine as a razor, I still get bumps with those. I have come up with a plan for my weekly cuts, man. Don't cut/shave for looks, but for comfort throughout the week. Smh. Now, on to the WAHL's.

This is my second week using these. They did not disappoint. Now, I have to break it down to two categories. Head/Scalp, Neck/Throat. Before moving on, take into consideration that the climate/temperature was right. My Head/Scalp, Neck/Throat were not sweaty/oily nor excessively dry/ashy. For me, at least, it's not always the best thing to have a super ashy surface while getting a hair cut. It could damage ones skin.

Head/Scalp: First and foremost, I did not tamper with the knob that provides more or maximum power. I used the clippers for the first time last week and I wasn't impressed. I read a few reviews and saw where people stated to be careful with them because they're extremely sharp. What? They are not sharp. They're zero gap, but I couldn't even edge with them. At first, I tried going with the grain, and they were inferior to the T-Outliner's. Against the grain... questionable, because at that point, I'd already cut most of my hair in all kinds of directions trying to find out which one worked best. =} Week two, though, when I had a better idea of how they operate, they did a superior job going against the grain... not because they're sharper, but because they're NOT as sharp and get a fine enough cut without my having to worry about razor bumps. They have a bit of a dull sensation to them that gets the job done. Now, after going against the grain all over my entire head (clippers flat, like the shovel in the snow), I still needed a finishing touch with the Andis T-Outliner's (positioned like an edge, paint brush against the wall). Why? Because the WAHL's weren't sharp enough to get as close as I'd like. But that's not a problem. It didn't get caught in any hair traps like the T-Outliner's do, and I was able to cut the hair off my head/scalp in a matter of minutes! All that said, I'd give them 4 (or a very, VERY hesitant 4.5) stars for my head/scalp.

Neck/Throat: My skin is not as tough here, so I was taking a shot in the dark with these WAHL's. The Master Clippers are too irritating here and the T-Outliners are forbidden... Here goes. Doing the shovel-in-the-snow motion only, I first went against the grain, and then with the grain. I felt my Neck/Throat during both motions with my left hand and I felt no stubble!? What the heck is going on? Honestly? This is where these bad boys shine! I was so impressed how well they did with my neck/throat area. The shave was just right! It wasn't too close and the blades weren't too sharp. After this, I 'forgave' the clippers for their not-as-expected Head/Scalp performance! LOL!!! I truthfully did. Just to let you know how well they did, I got NO STING with the rubbing alcohol when I was done. NONE! I gave them a week, and no irritation. Don't cut/shave for looks, but for comfort throughout the week. They are superior to ANY pair of clippers I've ever owned for my neck/throat. I gave them 5 stars for my neck/throat and would give more if I could.

So, there you have it. Assuming it gets approved, this has to be the longest review I've written on Amazon. It's important to me that people get detailed opinions from readers and not some fake, made-up crap that'll point you in the wrong direction. Just to put it out there, I did oil the blades as directed and I don't have a third category for my beard because they do nothing for me there. The other two are all I need for that, plus these WAHL's have set blades that don't adjust (which is what drew me to them in the first place). I'm very happy with this purchase and give it 4.5 stars. Unfortunately, since I can't put in 4.5 stars on Amazon, I have to settle with 4 stars. Aaah, well! I'll give it a five. But do know that they're not perfect!! =/ I wish they had a cordless version that performed just as well. I'm glad I added these to my arsenal. There's something magical about the number 3... A proper triangle is the strongest and most balanced structure, third time's a charm, and now the third clippers. Though they're not the sharpest, they get the job done. (=

***There's a trick I learned years ago to ensure that your zero gap is appropriate. If you lightly pinch the upper and lower blade at the tip where they both meet, you'll feel an effortless, smooth movement between the two that'll even allow for them to flow between your pinch, and the slight opening between both of your 'pinched' fingers can move left and right like a swaying dance. If you put your finger on top of the blades (with the power off) and rub it across lightly, you'll feel no friction. However, if you have no zero gap, you'll feel friction either from an overlap or underlap of the blades. That's how you know they may need to be corrected. The WAHL's don't do that swaying dance. Thanks for reading!!! (=

Z.
top_rant.title: There's something magical about the number 3

top_rant.text:
I’m sure you can guess why I hate to say how impressive this is….the expensive price tag it has 😬 I have trouble spending that kind of money on myself unless it’s something really useful that I’m going to get a ton of use out of, and usually that will be a single purchase that will last me a long time. So, skin care definitely doesn’t qualify into that.

I am 36. A few years back I started incorporating an anti-aging skin care routine. I should have been doing it since my 20s BEFORE anything appeared (it is definitely easier to prevent things than to make them go away. Right? But, alas, here we are). I now have pretty prominent sun spots on my cheekbones from my younger years (If we only knew then what we know now). I also have some post acne marks - I get hormonal acne and when a pimple finally goes away it almost always leaves a red mark behind and it’s so hard to make them go away! I also have some scarring from picking at my pimples…again, from my younger years 🤦‍♀️ I know better now. Thankfully, pimple patches have became a thing and I can use those to stop me from picking. So, I haven’t gotten any new scars in a long time. But, I want the old ones to go away! I also have a few fine lines.

I have definitely found that a price doesn’t usually factor in on whether it’s going to have good, effective ingredients that are going to actually live up to its claims. I’ve found great affordable ones and crappy affordable ones, and I’ve found great expensive ones and crappy expensive ones. So, I have been in the line of thinking that you don’t need to spend a ton to get a product with good, effective ingredients. But, that might just be the cheap side of me that doesn’t like to spend much money on myself saying that. If a skincare product is expensive but ends up working really well, that would mean I’m going to have to start forking over that expensive amount often since skincare runs out pretty quickly. With this product, you get 1.8 oz/51 g of product and are supposed to use it 2x/day, so it will run out probably in a month or so. Costing $98.99 per bottle, that gets pretty expensive! So, usually when I get an expensive skincare product like this to try, I think I’m kind of hoping I don’t end up loving it since that would mean I’d have to continually re-purchase it 😂 I also expect a lot out of a product that costs this much. If it’s going to be worth that price tag, then it needs to live up to all the claims it makes! So, let’s see….

🔴Radiant skin? Yes, it does give me radiant skin. My skin has a nice healthy, glow to it. Not greasy or oily looking. Just healthy, hydrating, radiant looking skin.
🔴Re-plumped, youthful, and smooth skin? Yes. It hydrates the skin, which is going to give it a little of a smooth and plumped look. If you have more prominent lines, I don’t know if it would make a difference there. But, maybe it could help reduce the look of them over time. But, for my fine lines it makes a big difference. They are very fine lines so they are mostly only noticeable to me unless someone is looking closely. But, after using this for a week, it seemed like my fine lines were less noticeable when I looked. After using it for a month, I can hardly see them at all anymore. But, it can’t just be the product hydrating my skin that does that because I’ve tried many hydrating products and none of them seemed to have this effect. So, I definitely think it’s more than that.
🔴Protects against UVA/UVB damage? I can’t really say for sure. I can say I haven’t gotten any more sun damage, but I think that’s more the work of my sunscreen than the work of this. This does have human growth factors, antioxidants, and peptides which all help protect the skin. So, it could help. But, I wouldn’t depend on this for my UVA/UVB protection.
🔴Instant visible results? This is a claim which is kind of odd. It says “INSTANT VISIBLE RESULTS” but then is followed by, “With regular use, you can make your skin look hydrated, smooth, and youthful. Our face cream can enhance skin shine in a short period.” Then, the next bullet point, the last sentence says, “It will help to smooth and blur the appearance of vertical wrinkles after only 7 days of use.” So, it’s kind of confusing. It says it provides “instant visible results” but then says “with regular use…,” “…in a short period.,” and “…after only 7 days of use.” So, it kind of contradicts itself a little there. I didn’t have instant results with my fine lines. I honestly wasn’t expecting it either. Most of the time if they give an instant effect of some sort it has an ingredient like caffeine, which will give an instant effect of reduced puffiness and darkness around the eyes, but is also only temporary. Usually, with instant results it means temporary results. Of course, I guess results from skin care are always temporary in a sense, since you have to continue to use them to continue getting the same results. Anyways, my point is, my fine lines reduced and even got to the point where I can hardly see them, but it wasn’t instantly. After using it for a week, they definitely were way less noticeable to me, so that is true. After using it for a month, I hardly see them at all. So, I do feel like with regular use this is effective, and I do feel it worked in a short time. I just wouldn’t say it’s instant. Instantly after I apply it, I do have hydrated, healthy looking skin that is soft, smooth, and has a glow to it that isn’t greasy or oily, just healthy and radiant looking. So, while confusing, I do feel the claims are correct. Just, definitely be aware this isn’t going to produce an instant reduction in fine lines (though it has worked better and in a shorter amount of time than other things I’ve tried!). Just make sure you aren’t expecting fine lines to be gone after one use of this.
🔴Lightweight and non-sticky? Absorbs into skin quickly? Leaves no residue behind? Yes, yes, and yes! This isn’t thick at all. It glides onto the skin easily and absorbs quickly and leaves behind no residue, sticky or tacky feeling. It leaves soft, smooth, hydrated, plumped up skin.
🔴Protect skin surface lipids from oxidative stress and strengthen the skin barrier function? Again, this isn’t really something I am going to really know for sure. But, it does have some great ingredients that are known to do exactly that, so I do believe it.
🔴Safe for all skin types - dry, oily, combo, sensitive, and mature? I would agree with this, though I obviously can’t say for sure since I am only one skin type - normal to dry, more on the dry side with some extremely dry areas. But, it seems very gentle and I don’t see anything in the ingredients the ingredients that would irritate sensitive skin. It is fragrance free too. It doesn’t seem like it would be too much for oily skin either.

Overall, I think this is amazing! I am seriously impressed with the results so far I’ve had after such a short time, so I’m really excited to see what continued, regular use will do!

SOME INFO ON GROWTH FACTORS & THE ONES IN THIS PRODUCT….

If you’re new to growth factors, they are one of the “it” skin care ingredients right now. They encourage new skin cell growth, boost collagen and elastin, reduce wrinkles, improve texture, reduce hyperpigmentation, etc. Like collagen, GFs are proteins that naturally occur in our skin. They’re manufactured by ALL cell types. In the skin, this includes the outermost epidermal layer and the deeper dermis, which includes keratinocytes on the surface, and fibroblasts (collagen factories) and melanocytes (pigment-making cells) below. They tell cells to do things like grow, divide, make more collagen and elastin, or increase blood flow. The amount of GFs we’re naturally born with decreases as we age, which is part of the reason for loose, sagging skin and wrinkles. Add that to outside threats, like sun exposure and pollution, and the process is exacerbated. But, when GFs are applied regularly for several weeks, they can act as a sort of replacement therapy, restoring all of the normal signals that support healthy skin when we’re young.

Now, it has amazing benefits. But, the major question a lot of people have when it comes to growth factors is - where do they come from? How are they sourced?

GFs in skin care are either from human stem cells or synthetic (bioengineered in a lab). Stem cells from any source - skin, fat, bone marrow, umbilical cord - can produce GFs that bring about a variety of different cell types in the body. They take their cues from surrounding tissue to become those types of cells, so if you put them on the skin, they help generate new skin cells.

You might have heard of epidermal growth factor (EGF), which is a growth factor that comes from human foreskin cells. You probably heard about the famous “penis facial” that went viral after she showed her Hollywood EGF Facial that was loved by celebrities. It used EGF harvested from LAB-GROWN foreskin cells. There’s another favorite EGF serum that uses neonatal foreskin fibroblasts for the EGF, but it can be sourced from placenta, bone marrow, and umbilical cord cells too.

With GFs there are plant-based alternatives. They are made from barley. It is said to be “human-like” in that it is active, potent, and has been scientifically proven effective.

In the ingredient list, it will just list the growth factor used. It doesn’t specify how it is sourced. So, if you want GFs that are plant-based, you’ll need to do some research for a brand that uses plant-derived, vegan GF.

So, if you feel uncomfortable with human-derived GFs, you want to look for an option using plant-derived GFs.

This has 2 types of of growth factors in it - Human Adipose Stromal Cell Conditioned Media and Human Neonatal Fibroblast Conditioned Media. When growth factors are followed by “conditioned media” that means they cover not one, but a mix of GFs derived from some cells grown in the lab. It’s basically a growth factor cocktail for your skin. In the US, human-derived GFs are all okay. In the EU, human cell-derived growth factors are illegal, so GF products available in the E.U. usually use a plant source (for example, barley).

With the “conditioned media,” they differ by the type of cells that the the GF mixture comes from.

With the “Adipose Stromal Cell Conditioned Media,” it is adipose-derived stem cells (ADSC), which are cells that differentiate later into fat cells. These cells are known to secrete various GFs that have significant wound healing and antioxidant properties.

When it comes to “Human Neonatal Fibroblast Conditioned Media,” I honestly couldn’t find anything specific to the “neonatal” part of it. I found information on “Human Fibroblast Conditioned Media,” which is derived from human fibroblast cells, which are the VIP skin cells for collagen production. But, it was missing the “neonatal” part. So, my guess is that it is fibroblasts from neonatal tissue?

• My resistance became so low, I became highly sensitive & allergic to many different things, from foods, to the environment, to all types of medications, plus having different types of autoimmune conditions from constant chronic stress causing additional & multiple health conditions which have no cure, i.e. fibromyalgia, ME/cfs chronic fatigue.

• I used to work in the medical field back in the '70s…. and never in a million years would have even considered 'natural or holistic 'even a possibility, until the later 70's. I had the belief that modern medicine was Thee Answer to everything!!! But it was far from the truth then, and to many degrees…. it is still true now!!!
• Modern medicine has come a long way, but it still needs room for improvement, to be more up-to-date, by not dismissing patients with chronic health conditions that has no cure; even people with dysautonomia like my daughter, also patients with reactivated EBV, etc...

ADDED THOUGHTS with important snippets:
• Perpetual symptom suppression and the lack of root cause resolution has resulted in a continued deterioration of these integral aspects of healthcare and widespread dissatisfaction among patients and practitioners alike.
_____________
Holistic Medicine
The methodology of Holistic Medicine is a treatment focused on mind, body, and spirit with an outlook that they are all interconnected. The ultimate goal is to bring mind, body and spirit into coherence. If this can be accomplished then the idea is the absence of disease and the presence of optimal health should usually follow.

Naturopathic Medicine
This medicine practice is best identified as the "healing power of nature." This model possesses a a broad spectrum of practice and a wide variety of therapeutic modalities. Some naturopaths will adhere to a strict practice of “nature cure” focusing only on diet, lifestyle modifications, detoxification and other natural interventions. Other naturopaths will offer additional non-conventional modalities such as acupuncture or homeopathy. At the other end of the spectrum are naturopathic physicians who extensively manipulate the body’s physiology and biochemistry by means of botanicals, nutraceuticals and pharmaceuticals. A majority of naturopathic practitioners incorporate pieces of all these elements in their practice and care for their patients.

Integrative Medicine
This model of healthcare is an integration of conventional with non-conventional or alternative modalities (such as herbs, homeopathy, chiropractic, acupuncture etc.).
The approach and thought process of the integrative practitioner may still be reductionistic with a strong emphasis placed on characterizing disorders by diagnosis as in the conventional medicine model, albeit with a greatly enlarged toolbox for evaluations and treatments that are available through non-conventional modalities.

The world’s leading proponent of integrative medicine, Dr. Andrew Weil packages it up nicely by saying, “Integrative medicine is a healing-oriented medicine that takes account of the whole person (body, mind & spirit), including all aspects of lifestyle. It emphasizes the therapeutic relationship and makes use of all appropriate therapies, both conventional and alternative.”

In short, Integrative Medicine is holistic because it uses a non-conventional medicine toolbox - including naturopathy - and it uses a conventional medicine toolbox. However, this enlarged Integrative toolbox may or may not be applied to identifying the root cause of the illness. All Integrative practitioners view the patient holistically and the most effective ones will seek out the root cause of the illness, but root cause resolution is not a prerequisite to practicing this model of healthcare.

Functional Medicine
Functional Medicine is a personalized and integrative approach to healthcare which involves understanding the prevention, management and root causes of complex chronic disease. Functional Medicine has taken the best aspects from all the models discussed above and offers the most comprehensive and effective approach to healthcare in the 21st century.

Licensed Functional Medicine practitioners usually come from a Conventional Medicine model background and integrate over to Functional once they come face to face with serious chronic diseases and are looking for long lasting effects to chronic, more complex diseases.

While Conventional has made waves in acute illnesses, when dealing with chronic complex medical issues Functional Medicine employs a systems biology approach that views the person within a holistic framework and their problems within a biological network.

Functional Medicine utilizes the most current scientific knowledge regarding how our genetics, environment and lifestyle interact as a whole system to diagnose and treat diseases based on patterns of dysfunction and imbalance – without necessarily treating the specific disease. Functional Medicine treats the person who has the disease, not the disease the person has.

Determining the root cause of the illness is an essential component of this model to the extent that a physician who does not practice in this manner is not practicing Functional Medicine. Symptom suppression is only used as a temporizing measure while seeking the root cause and when clinically necessary to optimize function of the patient.

A perfectly healthy human being is incredibly complex. Add to that an illness or imbalance and that complexity grows exponentially. All of the approaches discussed have their merits, but the obvious choice would be an approach encompassing all of them while seeking the root cause of the illness using Systems Biology to unravel the networks that make up who we are.

(Sorry in advance for the super long review) I absolutely LOVE this & feel that any rating less then a 5 star about the system & product itself, could have been avoided by adjusting a few things. So I decided to share a few tips that I've learned through trial & error that I wish I would have known when I first received this!!
TIPS:
OIL & WATER DON'T MIX!! For those who have never airbrushed before, you probably have your own makeup routine that you're used to...Mine personally, since I'm in my 30's, includes primer before makeup to create that "perfect canvas" & fill in my fine lines. My first HUGE mistake was not realizing that since this is water based & most primers contain silicone, this makeup would slide right off. This came in the mail & I called my daughter & mom to come over so we could try this out & ALL of us put primer on before we started to airbrush & all of us said, wow, this sucks, send this back...(Sorry, being honest). The make-up caked, bubbled, & just touching it took the makeup right off. We all looked like zebras & were not happy, actually very disappointed. So they left & I researched & researched what the heck we did wrong & decided to try this with nothing underneath...no problems, but, I still was aware of my fine lines. After researching more, I learned about the oil & water theory, bought a water based primer, moisturized underneath & PERFECTION! Flawless makeup, smudge, rub proof, just perfect & exactly what I had hoped for when I originally purchased this!
MOISTURIZE!! Airbrush makeup tends to have a dry/matte look to it, porcelain skin. If your skin isn't moisturized underneath, you may get some dry looking patches. Again, stay away from anything w/ silicone in it & get a good water based moisturizer...this makeup will look even & stick/cling to it giving you makeup that stays put for 12+ hours!
WANT GLOWING SKIN? If you're not a fan of the porcelain/matte look & want your skin to have more of a glow to it, like you're not wearing makeup...First start off using the highlighter all over your face, then put on your foundation....you will get compliments on your skin & this will look more natural, like youre not wearing makeup, your skin but better!!
TURN DOWN THE PRESSURE!! Another huge mistake I suffered through when I first received this, why/how are they saying 5-7 drops of makeup for your whole face??? I was using 20 drops & still running out before I was done! My first thoughts again, negative, frustrated, ready to send this back & again thought I'm doing something wrong & researched. After turning the dial (speed) down about 1/2 way....I get the perfect speed by turning the machine on, adjusting the dial to the highest by spraying air on my hand & then turning it down until I still feel a lot of air, but not at the highest, this is the setting I use (middle). 5-7 drops is all I need for a perfect base.
DON'T PULL THE TRIGGER ALL THE WAY BACK!! I'm putting this as a tip only because I may be odd, but my first instinct was that unless you pull the trigger on the gun all the way back, it won't work. Pulling it all the way back will cause you to use MAX makeup & your makeup will look caked on. Try pulling the trigger 1/2 way back & see how big of a difference this will make!!
DON'T STOP MOVING!! The trick to makeup not caking or building up super thick is to constantly be moving your hand/the gun!! If you stop moving your hand while spraying you're letting makeup layer up in one spot & it may look caked on. Do a fast constant moving motion in light layers for the most natural & even look!!
DON'T SPRAY TOO CLOSE!! This is just practice...I can say to use the sheet of paper they include for practice & that's probably the best advice, but, you also need to get a feel for spraying this on your actual face. My best advice for this is to start further away from your face then move closer...Move in until you start seeing the coverage that you like.
SPRAY & WAIT A MINUTE!! For some reason, when you first spray this makeup on, as it dries, it will change after a minute or two...(The color, the coverage). Spray on a thin layer & wait a minute or two, look at the coverage & color & then repeat. I was spraying & then putting another layer on right away.....BAD IDEA!! Almost like painting your nails & then trying to put another layer on before the first is dry. Not even, will never dry, ect.
CONTOURING/HIGHLIGHTS: The bronzer included with this is super pigmented, when using the bronzer, turn the pressure/speed almost all the way down when spraying it & try to do a sweeping action along your cheekbones or wherever your contouring. If the pressure is too high, its dramatic & may cause you to start all over again, washing off all your makeup, wasting makeup & time & its frustrating!! For the highlighting I would say the same, turn the pressure down....the highlights are an easier fix though, if you get too close, spray a little of your foundation over it to tone it down.
QUICK FIX FOR A MESS UP Since this is water based, if you mess up, get too close when trying to conceal something, bronzer/blush too dark, ect....I have found that if you have a beauty blender, soak it in water then squeeze out the excess, so the blender is just damp, you can lightly dab your mess up with the damp blender & it will blend your makeup in..DONT RUB!!. Rubbing with the wet blender will rub off your makeup..once it's blended in a bit or the blender takes off a bit off the excess, then spray a light layer of your foundation color over top once its dry. This should cover up/blend nicely.
CREASING UNDER EYES I'm adding this tip because as it wasn't a problem with my makeup, it was when applying this to my mom. She's in her 70's, wears glasses & anytime I spray near her eyes, she has the tendency to squint really tight...so basically, when she opens her eyes, she has makeup laying on top of where she was squinting that didn't cover lines. There's a few different ways I found to correct this...The damp blender....Dabbing the foundation in lightly right after applying it...then once covered lightly, spraying over her entire face from further way, seems to do the trick nicely. Turning the pressure down to low, so there's not as much air blowing out when spraying the eye area & you're not squinting as tight....you can basically keep your eyes open when the pressure is that low because it doesn't feel like a blast of air. Also, applying an under eye concealer after moisturizing, before foundation, then just normal light layers over then entire face. The foundation will blend over the concealer. Another suggestion is if you see creasing under your eyes once you're done airbrushing & all of your makeup has dried...take setting powder & lightly brush this area & it will all blend in.
CANT FIND YOUR SHADE This was a big problem for me at first until I heard about back bubbling your gun to mix colors. All you do is put in a few drops of different shades...put your finger over the nozzle that it sprays out of & pull your trigger back slightly. The foundation will start to bubble & mix up. Let this settle for a minute & you've got a whole new shade to try. You can also do this with adding a drop or two of the highlight to your normal shade for a more glowing/dewy look if your not a fan of the matte/porcelain look.
IN A HURRY If your in a hurry, running late ect & want your makeup to set quicker between layers, put fewer drops into your gun, do a light layer, then once the gun is empty, go over your foundation w/ just air, it will dry it/set it quicker & you won't have to wait a few minutes between layers & can then apply more as needed without your makeup looking caked on!!
CONCEALING FIRST If you have a scar, acne scab, mole ect that's a lot darker then your skin, airbrushing will conceal it by going in closer & concentrating on that area, but, putting too much makeup on it to cover might make that area look cakey until you get the hang of airbrushing....I have dabbed a little concealer on an area, then went over with the airbrush foundation & it covered perfectly
PLAY WITH COLORS TO MAKE YOUR OWN EYE SHADOWS I have found the darker shades & the blushes also work great as an eye shadow....what I've done is taken a darker shade, added a drop or two of the highlight for a sparkle effect, back bubbled it until it was mixed, turned the pressure down to low so you can get in closer & concentrate on that area & used this as a eye shadow. You can get paper to hold over your brow or under your eye if your worried about the color going elsewhere, most cosmetic stores & amazon also sell eye shadow stencils if you want to invest, but paper works great for me. I usually make more of the darker then needed, say for the eye crease & outer corners of your eye, then once those areas are done, I put a drop or two of light foundation into it to go over the brow bone area...that way I have the same color but just lighter/darker...if that makes sense. Once i'm done with the color that I like, rinse any excess out of the gun, wait until its dry then add about two drops of highlight & spray over my entire eye area from further away/low pressure & it makes my eyes pop & gives my upper cheekbone area a nice glow....just a suggestion
POWDERS AFTER ARE OK!! If you just want to create a base using airbrush & get a flawless foundation look, once this is dry, you can use your normal powder blush, or powder highlight/bronzer for contour, setting powders ect, afterwards. You don't have to choose between one or the other & it took me a little while to realize this when I bought this...I'm used to contouring/highlighting & until I got the hang of doing it with the airbrush, I did it using powders & there was no problem at all with airbrush coming off or it not sticking to the airbrushed foundation...just apply your powders lightly with brushes

My overall review...in short, lol, I will not go back to using normal foundation after using this!! My skin seems healthier...since switching to airbrushing, I no longer have breakouts & even after I wash this off, my skin feels softer in general. Aeroblend customer service is the best! If you read this review, you've seen I've had my issues & they were there, nice & had no problem at all answering questions or going above & beyond to make sure I was happy. I would highly recommend purchasing this!!
