top_rant.text:
I waited 4 months after receiving this chair to write this review. I wanted to make sure I gave an extensive review because somethings were contradictory between pictures/reviews and I wanted to make sure I fully understood this chair and what it could and couldn’t do prior to writing the review.

I was leery spending so much money on a chair that I couldn’t sit in and test prior to purchasing. I relied on all of the Amazon reviews and decided with so many amazing reviews, it has to be good (and thus, why I feel compelled to write this review, hopefully paying it forward). I ordered from Walmart because even though it was a little more expensive, the chair + Walmart’s 4-year warranty was cheaper than Amazon’s chair + 3 year warranty. My review is also on Walmart’s site as a verified purchase.

Delivery:
There is no white glove service and this chair is heavy and awkward. The delivery guy came and they are contracted to leave it at the curb but I told him I’d give him $20 if he brought it to my front door and I’d do the rest (I have a short cobblestone driveway…don’t make these guys crazy or tip them accordingly). I’m a single woman, 5’4” 115 pounds…so he was kind enough to actually bring it into my living room. I gave him $20 and he was on his way. Kudos to him and the shipping company. I was happy with how this worked out but know they aren’t required to do this and YMMV. I’m not sure what I would have done otherwise…maybe recruited the neighbor kids?

Using it:
The box with the chair inside was on tile in my living room. I cut the box from around the chair, pulled away the styrofoam and was able to push the chair about 7’ into its spot (this almost killed me…and I was ready for a massage). All you need to do is plug it in. No assembly and no cords to attach. I plugged it in, sat down and turned the power on (unaware of what was going to happen next). NOW…if you’ve sat in one of these chairs before, the quality and pressure varies (in ways I can’t even explain…but if you know, you know). I had been to a few stores, tested LOTS of chairs. I put my feet and arms in their slots and once it scanned me, I selected Stretch (which is supposedly Thai Stretch). The program started and the air pressure on the arms pinned me in…to the point where I couldn’t escape…even if I wanted to. The top of the chair started going one way and the bottom the other. For a split second I thought I was going to die…Thai stretched apart. LOL The pressure was tight and the stretching intense. I wanted it to stop but couldn’t do anything about it because I was pinned into this chair and alone (There’s no way to hold on to the remote or make it stop once the arm air pressure pins you in…I would say the only “con” of this chair but EASY to overlook with all of the “pros”). Looking back, it’s hilarious and I have laughed a lot about it whenever I tell the story (maybe I’m a little dramatic). The arm pressure finally released and I stopped the chair. All this happened in probably 2 minutes but not knowing what to expect, it was startling and seemed like an eternity. Ha (Note: Don’t start with Stretch). I went back and did the relaxing massage after that and just let it run its course for 15 minutes (the standard preset). It’s equivalent to any of the $3500-5k massage chairs I tested at various stores.

The Track:
This is not true 3D. Anyone who has done research and tested chairs will know 3D tech can vary the pressure in/out. This does not do that. You can vary the speed…but not the pressure. Also, don’t be misled, the track goes just under your butt. It does give you a great butt massage but don’t think it’s going to massage your hamstrings. It doesn’t make it that far. It stops right about at the crease between your butt and hamstring. Still amazing. But the reviews and pictures made it awfully ambiguous. I am here to 100% say, no hamstring massage. I tested a lot of chairs and it wasn’t until the $4-5k chairs where the track went down that far and I got a hamstring massage; even then, the extended track didn’t go that far down the hamstrings.

The Remote:
At first, like all the other reviewers, I thought the remote could use some work. But you’re getting a $3-5k chair for $1500…you need to overlook some of this nonsense for what you’re paying. And once you get the hang of it, it’s not that difficult. I uploaded a lot of pictures of the different remote screens because it's really not as bad as all the reviews say.

The chair automatically starts once powered on. You need to hit the Home button to select:
Auto, Airbag, Manual, Other & Settings

In Auto, you can select from 1 of 6 pre-programmed massages:
Relaxing, Mild Mode, Strong Mode, Sleep Mode, Stretch and Healthy
All are great (I even like Stretch now that I know what’s going on…if you’re concerned, just leave one arm out of the airbag slot and on the remote so you can power off if it gets too intense…until you know what to expect.)

In Airbag, you can adjust:
All Airbag: Auto/Manual (defaults to Auto with all the Auto programming)
If Manual is selected:
Shoulder: on/off
Arm: on/off
Cushion: on/off
Leg: on/off
Airbag Adj: Mid/Max/Mix (adjusts pressure…but it’s not a huge difference. I’m small and lean so it doesn’t seem like there’s much difference. Bigger people may see a difference in the pressure.)

In Manual, you can adjust:
Mode: Kneading, Knocking, Clapping, Shiatsu, Knead & Knock, Knead & Clap
Speed: Mix, Half or Max
Range: Mid or Narrow
Parts: Full, Neck, Waist, Hip and Fixed
The great thing about “Fixed” is you can select a Mode, Speed and Range along with Fixed, go back to the main screen and use the arrows up/down to go to that “spot” that ails you and it will stay there until you change it or the time runs out.

In Other, you can adjust:
Roller: On/Off
Roller Speed: Mid/Max/Mix
Hot: On/Off (the heat is AMAZING. I love it! Not too hot…it’s perfect IMHO. If you think it’s too hot, put a light blanket behind your back.)
Bluetooth: On/Off (If you’re buying this massage chair for the speakers, I don’t know what to say. I’m a girl. Speakers are speakers. I don’t really use this feature because I massage in silence or while watching TV. It’s a bonus as far as I’m concerned.)

In Settings, you can adjust:
Time: 5/10/15/20/25/30 (The time stays on the last time selected. So if my last massage was 25 minutes and I power down, when I power back on, it will automatically revert to 25 minutes. Once I unplug however, it reverts back to 15 minutes.)

Like others have said, it would be great if you could save a manually configured program. However, in fairness, I did not find any chair in all my research that could do this. $8-15k+ chairs may be able to do this (not entirely sure though) but that’s too rich for my blood! LOL

Is the chair comfortable to sit in without the massage on…just as a zero gravity chair? I don’t think so. I can feel the rails in the back. Everyone says it’s cause I don’t have any “cushion”. I am on the leaner side so I don’t see this being comfortable. I have folded up a blanket and put it against the back and that helps. But every 30 minutes, the chair powers down and goes back to the floor. You have to power it back on, turn everything off and put it back in the zero gravity position. So if you’re thinking about taking a zero gravity nap, it’s not happening…or it’s not going to be more than 30 minutes.

What if the pressure is too intense? At first, I folded up a little blanket and put it where I needed to buffer the pressure. I think your body just needs to get used to it. I don’t need the buffer now.

The pressure isn’t enough? I’m not sure what these people are talking about. I am on the leaner side but I’ve been an athlete my entire life. I’m 45 and still super active. I like a deep massage that after I feel like it beat the crap out of me. LOL Okay, it doesn’t beat the crap out of me, but you know what I mean. All my friends (of all sizes) LOVE this chair!

What if I remove the cushion over the part that massages my neck? You’ll just get a more intense massage. That cushion buffers your neck from the massager. I have big guy friends that love it with that cushion flipped over to the back side of the chair (or you can unzip it and remove it). At 5’4”, I don’t think I get a great neck massage anyways. I’m just not tall enough. I can feel it and I have to wiggle my way up if I want a deeper massage. Luckily, my neck isn’t my issue, it’s my back, specifically low back and this hits the spot perfectly.

The feet: The foot rollers are awesome! It tickles but it feels amazing. I sat in a chair with 1 roller and it was a joke compared to this one. Not sure how many rollers there are, but it is amazing. One of my friends has size 12 shoe. He said his feet fit but his toes were scrunched. Not uncomfortable, but I think anything larger than that may be pushing it.

The chair beeps at me even though it’s powered off? They recommend you unplug it. I was lucky enough to plug it into an outlet that I could turn on/off with a wall switch. But yes, if you don’t do that, it will beep at you to unplug it.

And yes, it is the truth. 4 months after getting this chair, my sister bought one for herself (never having used mine as she lives in another state). I just haven’t stopped raving. If you want a chair, I personally don’t think there is a better value out there. This is not a normal $1500 chair. Again, it’s equivalent to a $3500-5k massage chair (minimum).

Just buy it…

I am fourteen years old and,I have used and tried all of the Wen cleansing conditioner products, from Lavender, to Tea Tree, the Pomegranate, and now to this stuff. Here's what sums this product up: I bought this "cleansing" conditioner about 3 months ago, and have used it for 2 months. For one thing, this DID NOT "cleanse" my hair as people are saying it does. It seemed like it put more oil in my hair, if anything. When I put it onto my head in the shower, it felt like I put a ton of bacon grease into my hair and onto my scalp. I also have severe asthma, combined with chronic bronchitis, and sinus issues, and , every time I would use this I would burst into a full-blown wheezing attack. Anyways, when I was rinsing it out, it felt SO OILY...no matter how much I rinsed it wouldn't come out! I also used under the amount of pumps that I was supposed to use. I originally had to use 17 pumps in all, but, to test if it would make my hair oily like other reviews said, I only used 8 pumps. I only used 8 PUMPS and my hair was a flat, grimy, dull, greasy mess! I used this for 2 months straight, this time using the correct amount of pumps and following ALL THE INSTRUCTIONS, and, my hair was never so unhealthy. People thought that I was actually ill. Even my hairstylist laughed when I told her I used Wen. I felt so ashamed of myself. Then, I found the Suave Naturals shampoo line and the Suave Professionals Rosemary Mint shampoo, and, WOW!, they are AMAZING!!. With one use of the Suave Rosemary Mint, it had removed all of the grime and grease that the 2 months of using Wen left behind..and the shine...unbelieveable! The same happened with the Suave Naturals line, after using that as well, my hair was shiny, sleek, and clarified. My hair has never been this healthy in a long time. My friends (who use Wen) ask me "what shampoo do you use on your hair? It looks so shiny and healthy!", I just simply reply, "Suave", and they just look at me, dumbfounded. Even strangers ask me that same question, and I reply, "Suave". My tips are, give the cheaper shampoos a chance first, like Suave, White Rain, and V05. They may just work!! Suave really knows how to make a shampoo! That's all I have to say. And, yesterday, I was reading a beauty magazine, and..guess what shampoo line got voted the best?? SUAVE!!!! I was laughing for about 2 minutes..(the next best shampoo was Herbal Essences Drama Clean, for any of you who like Herbal Essences) Also, here is another thing I have to put in. If Wen was actually a legitimate product, then it should be sold in hair salons as well, not by their crummy infomercials and QVC. The only question that I was asking myself while using all of the Wen products was exactly this: "WEN WILL THEY WORK????".....

And, also, to support my reasonings, I did a little research on various Wen complaints. In fact, here's what TotalBeauty.com had to say about Wen, which was on their second worst hair care product list besides Garnier Fructis:

The Worst: No. 2: Wen Sweet Almond Mint Cleansing Conditioner, $28

Why: Readers say this product gives you "mediocre results for the money." Even after using the product as directed, there is "no noticeable change" in the look and feel of their hair, and one reader says it even dried it out more. "I could not do anything with my hair. It would not hold style or shape," she says. "It actually made my hair worse," says another reader. "I purposely washed with my regular shampoo [a week later] and saw a big difference." Several readers say, "the scent takes some getting used to" and label it as "hype" that is "not worth the price."

Want some more evidence?? Here are some more reviews from Makeupalley.com as well:

Rachel604 on 4/12/2013 5:10:00 PM More reviews by Rachel604

Age: 19-24 Skin: Combination, Fair-Medium, Not Sure Hair: Blond, Other, Other Eyes: Hazel

After seeing the infomercial, I was excited to try a sulfate and detergent free shampoo. I'm going away for a month this summer and I won't be able to shower everyday and I was hoping that this product would get my hair out of the "habit" of getting shampooed everyday.

Packaging: The reason why I gave the packaging such a low rating is because of the amount of product that is given in a "month's supply." The 355ml bottle would be more than enough but the instructions say to use 16 pumps for medium length hair. I have been using it every other day for the past 3 weeks and I am already completely out of product.

Product: The almond mint scent was pretty light and didn't really stick to my hair. I would have almost rather had a stronger scent because my hair always felt and smelled dirty while I was using this shampoo. The consistency was really heavy and didn't lather so I found myself needing the full 16 pumps just to be able to get it all over my hair.

How my hair felt: In short, my hair felt like crap.
I had just run out of a product I found at winners called muk which made my hair look and feel amazing. Winners stopped selling it so I decided to give wen a try. After my first use I noticed that there was a bit of residue left on my hair which made my hands feel waxy. After blow-drying my hair, it felt sticky and it was pretty tangly and somehow straighter than usual...almost looked like straw.
I spent extra time rinsing the product out of my hair after my first time using it but no matter how much I rinsed it (with my high pressure showerhead nonetheless) there was still this sticky, waxy residue on my hair. I resorted to tying it up most of the time and when I did have to wear it down, I would mix regular shampoo into it.

Overall this has not been a good month for my hair. It has been sticky, waxy, dull, limp and in a ponytail for most of the time...It also smelled pretty bad...

On the plus side, I have gotten out of the habit of shampooing so I guess wen did serve it's purpose for me even if I had to walk around for a month with dirty hippy hair.

I can't speak for everyone but I feel like this product would be better suited for people with extremely damaged hair. I can see it calming frizzy hair down because of the heavy formula and I'm sure it probably does make your hair healthier, but I started out with healthy hair already and I don't find that this product improved it's condition.

Here's another review.....

mmfoy on 3/25/2013 2:45:00 PM More reviews by mmfoy

Age: 25-29 Skin: Oily, Fair, Not Sure Hair: Brunette, Other, Other Eyes: Green

This is the first product I've ever purchased where I was so frustrated and annoyed by it that I rushed out to return it and felt incredibly happy having gotten rid of it.

I decided to try it since I've been wanting to cut down on the products I use. I read up on reviews and understood that there were very mixed reviews, but since they had it at my local Sephora, I suppose it couldn't hurt.

WRONG.

The first washing went really well. My hair looked fantastic and was easy to manage. That's the only positive thing you'll read me say.

Subsequent washings went TERRIBLE, even after adjusting how much product I put in (10-15 pumps just seemed absolutely ridiculous to me for the price). I would scrub, and scrub, and scrub it out of my hair, but even a few hours later, my roots would be looking greasy. The top of my hair would then start to follow suit and I would lose and volume I had. After coming home from work one evening, I saw how bad my hair looked in the mirror and I immediately washed it. My old Head and Shoulders washed all the gunk out and made my hair feel fluffy and bouncy, as it should be!

I was so unimpressed with Wen, not only for the price, but for what it did to my hair. My hair usually isn't very oily, so this was very disappointing to see how it affected my hair.

I will NOT be repurchasing, and I'm so glad I got this out of my house!

And another....

Age: 19-24 Skin: Combination, Fair, Not Sure Hair: Blond, Wavy, Medium Eyes: Blue

This company is the biggest scam, and not to mention, the conditioning shampoo is a cheap and damaging product!!! I purchased one order for the sweet almond mint conditioning shampoo, deep conditioner, and conditioning mist. They included a comb, styling cream and Endless Youth vitamins as a "free gift". Little did I know I just locked myself into $280 worth of unauthorized charges to my accts. Not only did they charge my account for multiple 6-month supplies without my request, but they got a hold of my NEW debit card number AND my OTHER debit card number and then charged BOTH accts. BEWARE when you place an order, they will continue to send products and you must cancel ASAP.

As for the quality of the conditioning shampoo, it is terrible. I am not just saying this because of my experience with the 8 unauthorized charges. My hair did not improve one bit by using the product, in fact, it became even more dry. I have thick hair with blonde highlights and was looking for some daily deep conditioning to improve the dryness. My hair showed now improvement and just got even worse. The only pro was the "free" comb I received good to use in the shower with conditioner on your hair. It is a waste of money and does hair no good, especially treated hair.

I could go on all day, with evidence of how bad Wen is for hair. If you want to buy this product, you better do your research first, because I found TONS of complaints about all the Wen hair care products. And, all of the products are not "natural" as it says they are, in fact, they contain the same ingredients as my Suave Rosemary Mint and Herbal Essences.

Time for more evidence! Here is the list of so-called "all-natural" ingredients found in this Wen product, taken RIGHT OFF THEIR WEBSITE:

Sweet Almond Mint Cleansing Conditioner:

Water, Aloe Vera Gel, Glycerin, Chamomile Extract, Cherry Bark Extract, Calendula Extract, Rosemary Extract, Behentrimonium Chloride, Stearamidopropyl Dimethylamine, Cetyl Alcohol, Emulsifying Wax, Panthenol, Trimethylsilylamodimethicone, Hydrolyzed Whole Wheat Protein, PEG-60, Almond Glycerides, Menthol, Essential Oils, Citric Acid, Methylchoroisothiazolinone, Methylisothiazolinone, Fragrance

Hmmmm...Let's see, it still contains Trimethylsilylamodimethicone, which is a sillicone, and sillicones are bad for hair, it has Citric Acid, which is a naturally occuring compound found in citrus fruits, and can weaken hair. The acid opens the hair cuticles, removing minerals fom the hair that can lead to discoloration and breakage. It also has Methylchoroisothiazolinone and Methylisothiazolinone, which can further damage the hair cuticle and the shaft, therefore causing hair loss. It can also cause severe allergic reactions as well.

All natural huh?? Let me tell you something, this cleansing conditioner contains THE SAME ingredients as a lot of drugstore shampoos out there, there is absolutely nothing special about Wen, that's final.

Also, they need to work on their customer service line as well. They are SO RUDE AND DISRESPECTFUL that even I, myself found it shocking. My mom tried to call nicely and cancel the other orders of Wen that I was suppossed to get, and they just backlashed her with a swarm of nasty remarks.

Chaz Dean obviously needs to work on two things:
1.) His products.
2.) His mediocre company.

Anyone want to know where my Wen Sweet Almond Mint Wen is?? I dumped it. Down the toilet. The sewer rats will gladly enjoy it now, as it might work for them.

I LOVE this line!!!😻😻 It will give you the most beautiful hair!! &&I have ordered from Amazon for the last 6years at least &it has always been consistent; REAL PRODUCTS; the real brand &high quality;; exactly what I expect EVERYtime!! Give it a try!!😻😻💙

In my experience you DEF DON’T have to be sketched to buy off of Amazon!!

I don’t love any other product as much as Biolage &&Bio’s “Hydra-source”!!
I have used a lot of different formulas: FiberStrong repair(6/10)(scent is💯);; the Exquisite Oil(now it’s called something else?)(8/10); && the color care(9/10)(&others) but the original Hydrasource has always been my favorite! I switched from the Lanza Healing Moisture//Healing Strength(in summer) to Biolage (it also helps it’s literally HALFthe price! TWICE the softness &3x the scent!)

The original Hydrasource is also available but I decided to give the “Ultra Hydra-source” a try && I’m super stoked I did! I LOVE IT!!!! I can’t say that enough!!
The formula really is different in my opinion.. it seems much “creamier” in consistency; but the conditioner feels lighter;; almost like fluffy feeling when you apply.. It is silky as it rinses &&it doesn’t seem to want to tangle as much!.. BUT it doesn’t weigh me down like some of the more “moisture//oils” shamp/condish products or like the all natural/organic products do!

I have been using this steadily for about 5/6mo now &&I think I’ll only get the “Ultra”now on!!! I normally wash my hair every day;; but I have been wash training to go down to 2-3days.. i can really tell a difference in my ends.. My roots don’t get as greasy as quickly && the ends are staying soft... usually by the end of the day/day 2 I’d have dry straw ends.. it’s improving every day but you will see a MAJOR DIFFERENCE in ONE WASH!!!

Give this a try because:
(1) It does NOT weigh your hair down!!! if you have fine hair;; especially if you have lots of hair(like me) or thick hair it will keep your ends soft & hydrated feeling until the next wash!
(2) I can tell the difference in ONE wash! I use other moisturizer combos but this is my favorite;; it’s so silky immediately! my hair feels like silk most of the day! You can feel it even while rinsing!!
(3) it’s easy on sensitive skin!! My whole family uses it! My son is 8&has VERY SENSITIVE skin && he has no problem with it! I usually use all natural for him;; but tried it while we were out && it made such a difference in his tangles! He has long; fine blonde hair like me && it gets so tangled && I love this for him so much it’s now what he uses!
&&it smells sooooooo GOOD!!

I use this on me but I mainly bought the “ultra rich” version for my husband to try.. he always complains that nothing works for his hair.. he has very thick dark blonde hair that tends to frizz (or “puff up” in his words) bc it’s between waves/curly/straight.. so for years he washes 1x/week.. I told him that’s gross && I’d find him something that worked! This Made his hair smooth; but without being “puffy”/floaty/ in his face &&without making it greasy or oily looking/feeling on his scalp.. he has sensitive skin also && we have been using mainly all natural products since our son was born &&for the last 2years I’ve tried almost EVERYTHING ON THE MARKET to find something for him... organic/natural and salon or drugstore products... he liked Biolage before but it wasn’t quite right..
the “ULTRA HydroSource” is the winner!!!
He is so glad I found him this! Thank goodness!! We all have long hair that tangles easily and it really helps!

**We also use the Hydrasource Leave-in**
moisturizer &detangling spray && it’s AMAZING if you have kids!! I water mine down as it’s a bit thick && a little too rich for me.. either with wet hands or add water straight to the bottle as it gets used! I have gotten in a routine where I feel like I NEED it! I use straight away after washes before I even towel dry my hair! That way it soaks in but it won’t get too much on too thick!

I know this is crazy/stupid long!! BUT I have had such a hard time with my hair especially after having my first baby &&as I get older!(just turned 30!) SO I told my story on the chance someone out there is like me&& needs help & hope!!
So I truely hope my review & story helps someone else out there; who has as much trouble as I have;; find this product &it works as well for you as it does for me &&my family!!

top_screaming.text:
MY MANNEQUIN HEAD CAME TODAY, A LITTLE EARLY, WHICH IS GOOD. IT WAS IN A PLASTIC BAG PACKED VERY NICELY IN A BOX WITH A MANNEQUIN HEAD TRIPOD I ORDERED ALSO. THE MANNEQUIN HEAD COMES WITH A FREE TABLE CLAMP & SOME HAIR STYLING ACCESSORIES WHICH IS NICE. ONE PARTICULAR ACCESSORY IS A SQUARE PIECE OF HOOK & LOOP ( VELCRO ) WHICH I HAVE NO IDEA WHAT THAT IS FOR. THE LITTLE BAG IT WAS IN SHOWS A PHOTO OF THIS PIECE OF VELCRO ON THE HAIR OF A MANNEQUIN BUT ALL THE WRITING IS IN ASIAN ( JAPANESE, CHINESE OR SOMETHING ) SO I HAVEN'T ANY IDEA WHAT TO DO WITH IT. IF SOMEONE CAN HELP ME WITH THAT I WOULD APPRECIATE IT. THERE IS ALSO ONE BAG OF HAIR TOOLS THAT SHOW HOW TO USE THE TOOLS IN PHOTOS BUT AGAIN, THE INSTRUCTIONS ARE IN CHINESE OR JAPANESE. I ALREADY KNOW HOW TO USE THOSE TOOLS BUT THE STEP BY STEP WAY OF GETTING TO THE DIFFERENT HAIRSTYLES USING THE TOOLS IS GONNA BE MY GUESS BECAUSE I ONLY READ ENGLISH. HOWEVER, I AM EXTREMELY SATISFIED WITH THIS "SILKY # 4" WITH 40 % HUMAN HAIR & 60 % SYNTHETIC HAIR. THE COLOR IS AS ORDERED - DARK BROWN & THE ADVERTISEMENT SAYS 24" TO 28", BUT IT IS LONGER THAN ADVERTISED, MINE IS 31 " FROM FOREHEAD HAIRLINE TO ENDS AT BACK OF HEAD
( I MEASURED FROM FORHEAD HAIRLINE, OVER TOP OF HEAD & DOWN TO ENDS ON BACKSIDE). THE HAIR IS VERY FULL & THICK & THE SCALP ON THE MANNEQUIN LOOKS JUST LIKE A HUMAN'S ANY PLACE I PART IT. NO BALD SPOTS. THE HAIRLINE AROUND FACE & NECK LOOKS JUST LIKE A HUMAN'S. THE HAIR FEELS REAL & LIFE - LIKE IN MY OPINION & SEEMS TO BRAID JUST LIKE HUMAN HAIR. I DO NOT PLAN TO USE HOT COMB OR BLOW DRYER ON IT, NOR DO I PLAN TO CUT IT OR PUT IT IN ROLLERS, AS I WILL BE USING MINE FOR PRACTICING BRAIDING & HAIRSTYLES ONLY. SO I CAN'T COMMENT ON HOW WELL OTHER TECHNIQUES MIGHT WORK ON THE HAIR. THERE IS NO SHEDDING, NOT ONE STRAND OF HAIR WHEN I COMBED IT OUT AFTER REMOVING IT FROM THE PLASTIC BAG. I DID DO, AS SUGGESTED BY MANUFACTURER, & GENTLY COMB THROUGH HAIR STARTING AT LENGTH ENDS & MOVED UPWARDS, RATHER THAN STARTING AT SCALP & MOVE DOWNWARDS. THEY SAID TO DO THE FIRST COMBING LIKE THAT SO I DID. AM SO PLEASED THAT IT HAD NO SHEDDING.
ADVERTISEMENT STATED IT HAS MINIMAL SHEDDING, BUT I HAVE NONE SO FAR. THOUGH I WILL SAY, AFTER I PLACED MANNEQUIN ON TRIPOD & PICKED UP PLASTIC BAG IT CAME IN (TO PUT OVER HEAD), I DID SEE A FEW STRANDS OF HAIR ON FLOOR THAT OBVIOUSLY WERE NOT MINE. SO I AM THINKING THEY FELL OUT OF BAG THE HEAD WAS IN. THEY COULD HAVE BEEN LOOSE HAIRS ON HEAD OR FELL OUT INTO BAG DURING SHIPMENT, I DON'T KNOW, BUT I HAD NO SHEDDING DURING COMBING & AFTER 1ST COMBING, I COMBED AGAIN FROM SCALP DOWNWARD TOWARD ENDS OF HAIR & STILL HAD NO SHEDDING AT ALL. THE HAIR REALLY DIDN'T NEED COMBING UPON OPENING BECAUSE IT WAS NOT TANGLED, BUT BECAUSE OF THE LONG LENGTH OF HAIR THAT HAD TO BE PACKED IN THAT PLASTIC BAG, IT WAS WRAPPED AROUND THE HEAD & ACROSS FACE, I GUESS TO KEEP STRANDS SMOOTH. THAT IS WHY I COMBED THROUGH IT. ALSO, HAVE READ SOME REVIEWS ON MANNEQUIN HEADS WITH HUMAN HAIR THAT SAID BUYERS FOUND DEAD NITS FROM LICE IN THE HAIR. WELL, I HAVE FOUND NONE IN MINE & THERE IS NO ODOR TO THE HAIR AT ALL. VERY CLEAN. I AM VERY SATISFIED WITH MY PURCHASE & WOULD HIGHLY RECOMMEND OTHERS TO BUY IT. IT IS PERFECT FOR WHAT I INTEND TO USE IT FOR.

I HOPE YOU WILL GET SOME GOOD SOLID INFO FROM MY REVIEW. FIRST, I WOULD LIKE TO SAY THAT I HAVE TRIED MANY ELECTRIC SHAVERS OVER THE PAST FEW WEEKS. I HATE SHAVING WITH A BLADE SO I WANTED TO FIND THE BEST SHAVER. I DO NOT WORK FOR PANASONIC NOR BRAUN. MY REVIEW IS HONEST AND I HAVE PUT ALOT OF TIME AND RESEARCH INTO THESE PRODUCTS, LOOKING FOR THE BEST SHAVER. I TESTED THE BRAUN PULSONIC 9595 SHAVER, AND ALSO THE PANASONIC VORTEX NANO ES 8109S FOR 30 DAYS. THE BRAUN I BOUGHT FOR 250 DOLLARS, THE PANASONIC VORTEX WAS ABOUT 130 DOLLARS. I ALSO USED THE PANASONIC 8077, WHICH IS DISCONTINUED AND ABOUT 40 DOLLARS ON THE INTERNET OR EBAY. I BOUGHT IT FROM SHARPER IMAGE FOR 99 DOLLARS MONTHS AGO. AGAIN, NOW WORTH ABOUT 40 BUCKS.

THE BRAUN DOES TONS OF MARKETING, MAKING BELIEVE THE PULSONIC MUST BE THE BEST SHAVER ON THE MARKET. IT IS A GREAT SHAVER AND REALLY WORKED PRETTY WELL. BUT THERE IS NO CONTEST. THE PANASONIC MODELS SHAVED MUCH CLOSER AND FOR ABOUT HALF THE PRICE. THE BRAUN DID OK ON THE NECK AREA BUT THE PANASONIC WON THE CONTEST. THE BRUAN WANTS YOU TO CLEAN THE UNIT EVERY FEW SHAVES, THAT GETS EXPENSIVE WHILE YOU MUST USE ITS CLEANING CARTRIDGE/SYSTEM. THE PANASONIC GIVES YOU THE SAME CLEANING SYSTEM BUT ALSO ALLOWS YOU TO USE THE WATER FROM YOUR SINK. ITS A WET AND DRY UNIT. THE PANASONIC BY FAR IS THE BETTER SHAVER AND MUCH CHEAPER PRICE WISE. I GUESS BRAUN CHARGES ALOT BECAUSE OF THERE EXPENSIVE MARKETING CAMPAIGN. IF I COMPARED THE PANASONIC 8077 TO THE MORE EXPENSIVE 8109, I MUST ADMIT, THEY SHAVE THE SAME. THE 8109 HAS NEWER TECHNOLOGY, FANCIER BUTTONS AND A CLEANING SYSTEM, BUT JUST COMPARING THE ACUAL SHAVE, THEY PERFORM THE SAME. YOU PAY MORE FOR THE 8109 BECAUSE IT HAS MORE BELLS AND WHISTLES. I HOPE THIS REVIEW HELPS. IN CONCLUSION, DO NOT FALL PREY TO ALL THE MARKETING HYPE BY BRAUN OR PANASONIC. JUST BECAUSE THE RAZORS GET FANCIER AND HAVE MORE BELLS AND WHISTLES, DOES NO MEAN THEY SHAVE BETTER. AGAIN, I WOULD CERTAINLY GO FOR THE PANASONIC MODELS OVER THE BRAUN. BEST WISHES WITH YOUR NEW SHAVER. I AM VERY HAPPY, MY RESEARCH IS OVER, I AM STICKING WITH THE ES 8077E, AND 8109E. IT OUT PERFORMS THE 250 DOLLAR EXPENSIVE MODEL BY BRAUN CALLED THE PULSONIC. HOPE THIS HELPS.

THIS IS A REVISED REVIEW I ALREADY DELETED MY FIRST REVIEW??. A THREE INCH STROP IS EASIER TO USE BUT THEN YOU NEED TO CONSIDER CUPPING. THE WIDER THE STROP THE MORE CHANCE OF CUPPING. SO THIS IS FINE AT 2.5 INCHES. I PURCHASED A BUFFALO BRAND STROP FROM ROYAL SHAVE BECAUSE I THOUGHT IT WOIULD BE BETTER BECAUSE OF THE PRICE TAG(150.00) THE HARDWARE LOOKED BETTER BUT THE LEATHER WAS TERRIBLE AND IT HAD A DEFECT IN IT. I RETURNED IT BUT THAT WAS A NIGHTMARE. THAT EXPERIENCE ALONE WAS A WAKE UP CALL AND I REALIZED WHAT A BARGAIN THIS STROP REALLY WAS.

IF YOUR NEW TO WET SHAVING WITH A STRAIGHT EDGE? YOU SHOULD KNOW THAT A STROP IS PROBABLY MORE IMPORTANT THAN A HONE. HAVING A STROP IS AN ABSOLUTE NECESSITY!!!!! A STONE IS NOT BECAUSE YOU CAN ALWAYS SEND YOUR RAZOR OUT TO GET SHARPENNED EVERY 3-6 MONTHS BUT YOU WILL NEED TO STROP YOUR RAZOR BEFORE AND AFTER EVERY SHAVE. AFTER SHAVING IS JUST A GOOD WAY TO MAKE SURE YOUR EDGE IS DRY BECAUSE THE STROP WILL WICK AWAY ANY MOISTURE IF STILL PRESENT. ALSO USE A PAPER TOWEL TO DRY YOUR BLADE.

WHEN YOU STROP YOUR RAZOR THERE IS NO NEED TO BE FAST ABOUT IT. TAKE YOUR TIME AND DO IT RIGHT AND TRY NOT TO CUT YOUR STROP. SEVEN ROUND TRIPS ON THE CANVAS SIDE AND FIFTY ROUND TRIPS ON THE SMOOTH LEATHER SIDE. LIGHT PRESSURE BUT NOT FEATHER WEIGHT PRESSURE. HANG YOUR STROP AT A HEIGHT SOMEWHERE AROUND THE HEIGHT OF YOUR BELLY BUTTON. SPEED WILL DEVELOPE OVER TIME. YOUR MAIN CONCERN IS DOING IT RIGHT. WHEN YOU NICK YOUT STROP ON THE SIDES IF IT IS A PROBLEM JUST SAND IT DOWN WITH SANDPAPER UNTIL IT IS SMOOTH. I HAVE NICKS ON MY STROP AND I DON"T DO ANYTHING TO REMOVE THEM.

GOOD LUCK AND REMEMBER THIS IS A GREAT STROP REGARDLESS OF YOUR EXPERIENCE LEVEL. ALSO YOU MAY WANT TO RUB STROP DRESSING ON IT TOO.

top_screaming.text: I WANTED TO TEACH MYSELF TO BRAID, SO I STARTED LOOKING FOR MANNEQUIN HEADS. AFTER READING MANY OF THE REVIEWS ABOUT THE HAIR SHEDDING AND NOT AS LONG AS THE PICTURE, I WAS A LITTLE HESITANT. HOWEVER, FOR THE PRICE AND TYPE OF HAIR I NEEDED, I TOOK A CHANCE. YES, IT SHEDS A LOT.....BUT, I'VE NOTICED THAT AFTER COMBING AND STYLING MULTIPLE TIMES (EVERY DAY ABOUT 4 TIMES A DAY, DOING BRAID STYLES FOR ABOUT A MONTH NOW SINCE I GOT IT) THE SHEDDING IS VERY MINIMAL. ALMOST LIKE IT HAS TO BE USED ENOUGH TO GET THAT SHEDDING STAGE OUT OF THE WAY. IT WILL ANNOY YOU AT FIRST,BUT IT DOES CEASE IF YOU'RE USING IT REGULARLY.  NOW, PEOPLE COMPLAINED ABOUT GREY HAIRS.  PERSONALLY, I JUST NEEDED HAIR TO BRAID, COLOR DID NOT MATTER. MY HEAD DID HAVE MAYBE 3-5 GREY HAIRS THROUGHOUT, BUT NOT A BIG DEAL TO ME AT ALL. IT WASN'T AN ISSUE AND IF YOU REALLY HATE THEM, YOU CAN CUT THEM OUT AND IT WOULDN'T AFFECT THE HAIR. THE SKIN TONE WAS A LITTLE DARKER THAN THE PICTURE, BUT I DIDN'T CARE. IT LOOKED EXACTLY LIKE HER FACE AND SKIN TONE HAD NOTHING TO DO WITH MY INTENTIONS. IF I NEEDED A MAKE-UP DOLL AND REQUIRED LIGHTER SKIN TONES, THEN I MIGHT BE A LITTLE MAD, BUT AGAIN, MY PURPOSE WAS STRICTLY LEARNING TO BRAID AND THE HAIR WAS MY FOCUS. THE HAIR REALLY IS A GOOD MATCH TO AFRICAN AMERICAN HAIR THAT'S BEEN BLOW DRIED OUT. SO, IF THAT'S THE TYPE OF HAIR YOU'RE LOOKING FOR, IT'S PRETTY CLOSE. THAT'S REALLY ALL I CAN THINK OF AS FAR AS THIS GOES. I WOULD DEFINITELY RECOMMEND THIS TO SOMEONE WANTING TO LEARN HOW TO BRAID. ONCE YOU GET PASSED THE SHEDDING PHASE, IT'S A GREAT TOOL TO HAVE. NOT ONLY DID I TEACH MYSELF TO BRAID, BUT I AM NOW MOVING ON TO LEARNING TO HOW TO WEAVE. I'VE HAD THIS ITEM NOW FOR ABOUT A MONTH AND I'M SO GLAD I GOT IT. THE PRICE WAS REASONABLE TOO. BASED ON OTHER REVIEWS TOO, I PURCHASED A HEAD CLAMP STAND. IT'S HANDY JUST IN CASE, BUT I HAVEN'T NEEDED IT AT ALL. I USED IT ONCE JUST TO TEST IT OUT AND FOR ME IT WAS EASIER HAVING THE MANNEQUIN HEAD BETWEEN MY LEGS. BUT, I FIGURED BETTER TO HAVE IT, THAN NOT. SO, OVERALL THIS IS A GREAT FIND. IF YOU'RE ON THE FENCE BECAUSE OF THE SHEDDING PROBLEMS, TRUST ME...IT GETS BETTER WITH USE AND IT REALLY IS A GOOD FIND FOR NOT A LOT OF MONEY!!! GOOD LUCK, I HOPE THIS HELPED SOMEONE!
top_screaming.title: GLAD I DECIDED TO TAKE A CHANCE!

top_exclaim.text:
My fiancé and I occasionally use the same shave cream/gel etcetera depending on various conditions. For the past 6 years I have been using Jack Black’s Supreme Cream to shave my legs, but recently I decided to try something a little less pricey...
You get what you pay for!
Not only did this item come with the box completely destroyed (basically desintigrated, like it was soaked through and run through a food processor) the tube was unsealed, no little foil button to confirm that I was the first to open the container.
My fiancé, whose skin is no where near as sensitive as mine tried it out first. Not very thick lather, even with a shave brush. Did not rinse clean, he said he used more hot water rincing his razor than he uses for a whole shower (surely an exaggeration).
However, after trying it on my legs I have to confirm that it does not cushion as Supreme Cream does, it does not rinse clean as supreme cream does, and it does not even come close to the lather WITHOUT a shave brush that supreme cream does.
Another HUGE letdown was the packaging. I’ll admit that if the box had been smashed, that’s okay, but if I were giving it as a gift, totally unacceptable. As for no seal on the tube....that’s unacceptable. Also, when I tried to return the tube I was told not to return the product and that I would receive a full refund. Since when is $5.36 a full refund when I paid $10.
NEVER EVER EVER EVER BUY FROM THIS COMPANY OR THIS SELLER. Product is terrible and that’s not all.
Update: Razors cost more to replace than the extra money for a better product, this gunks up the razor so bad its toast 2-3 times faster than usual.

top_five_star_obscene.text: It is so difficult to get chlorine out of thick hair and actually takes me a couple of days to finally not smell it. This shampoo is a wonderful invention. It strips your hair of all of the crap (which is something we should do anyway once in a while...but not too often or your hair will be crappy) and the chlorine is absolutely gone! I wasn't a big fan of the smell but what can you do? So I used it and then used my own shampoo afterward. Then I used the Ultra Swim Conditioner, which is a must because you have to put the nutrients back into your hair that it needs after stripping it down. top_five_star_obscene.title: It works!!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I was very apprehensive about purchasing this "unknown" product for my fiance who is very particular and already had a standard style electric shaver, but I'm so happy I took the chance! I gave this to him at Christmas, and now it is almost March so he's had a few months to really test it out.

He absolutely LOVES this thing!

He uses it on both is head and his face and says it's the most comfortable, best shaver he's ever had in his life (he's been shaving his head many, many years). The product information says it may take a man a few weeks to "adjust" to the shaving, but my man had no problem like that all, no chaffing, no problem, just a beautiful smooth head and face. He usually has it in his truck and can use it driving down the road on his way to a job. The battery life is amazing, he can go days on a charge!

I can't think of any complaints, he loves it, he loves me for buying it for him, what's to complain about? He hasn't tried the attachments yet (you can buy clippers and nose picker), but probably will buy them soon to try.

Thank you Bald Eagle Shaver!

UPDATE: Christmas 2016 only complaint is he didn't take care of it, threw it in his truck console despite my complaints that it would get broken..... and it got kicked around in there. One head broke off, he kept using it till a 2nd broke off. It didn't last a full year under these circumstances, but I do not blame the equipment. I did buy him the hard-case to keep it in, but clearly he never used the case. This shaver does not stand up to men throwing it in their work truck console full of junk they rifle through on a continuous basis :(

The price is right, and yes, I could order him a new one, but it really pissed me off that he didn't take care of something as nice as this that I had given him. I was a single mother of three when I bought it, and under those circumstances, it wasn't "cheap" to add that to my Christmas gift budget. We'd probably spank the kids if they tore up a hundred dollar toy, right? At least they'd get in trouble for mistreating the item.... good Lord he complains if they don't use the kick stand on their bike because it might get torn up by laying in the grass... but a grown man beat this around with tools and crap in a box in his truck and saw nothing wrong with letting it get tore up.

As much as he loved and bragged about the skull shaver, alas, it got no respect as fine tuned personal grooming unit. Maybe by next Christmas I'll be over my grudge and consider buying him a new one. I know he spends more than twice that a year on disposables now. Don't let that stop you from buying one though, it's still a great shaver.
top_five_star_obscene.title: This Shaver ROCKS!  Buy it for your Man!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I bought the Intensive Therapy at a big box store that starts with W, fell in love with it, came to Amazon to check out other items from the brand. This duo cost me less than the single bottle did at the store. I'm either giving my mom my extra IT or I'm saving it.

Background: I have extremely THIN peeling nails because of nutrient deficiencies that I need to work on. I do my nails a lot cause it's my passion, so my nails go through a lot of crap. But even when I take long hiatuses from painting them, they are still awful. I paint my nails 1-3 times a week, sometimes more, sometimes less. I've been using the IT for about 3 weeks, Foundation for about a week. My nails are about as bend-resistant as a generic solo cup if that makes sense. This is important to take note of.

First lets talk about Intensive Therapy. WOW this stuff is great. After 2 or 3 uses, I noticed my nails were growing like crazy, polishes were applying smoother, and my nails started to feel thicker. They're starting to feel like real nails. I'm amazed. I have tried practically every strengthening base out there with no results. I will never stop using this stuff. I apply it 1-2 times a day if I'm not wearing regular polish. If I am, I apply a coat over my polish every day like the box says. The key to this stuff is CONSISTENCY. Use it a lot!

Now for Foundation. I love it also. I haven't used it long enough to form too many opinions yet. But anyway. I have pretty ridge-y nails, so when I wear polish, you can see them and it ruins the look. They look ugly as you know what if I don't buff them, but that's bad for your nails. I applied this stuff and I was amazed. I looked at my nails from different angles and I hardly saw ridges. I also enjoy how it looks on it's own. It has a matte finish and makes my nails look thick.

Bottom line, I really love both of these. I'm gonna experiment with layering them. I won't try any other bases for a long time. Even if this stuff gets gummy fast, I won't mind, because it's RIDICULOUSLY cheap at this price. Each one is about $10 at the store, which is not economical to buy every other month. You get both of these for less than that! You get them for the price of two crappy non-prestige-brand base coats like a certain brand that rhymes with Rally Manson. I am officially Nail Tek's newest fan. I love you NT!
top_five_star_obscene.title: Freakin AWESOME stuff

top_five_star_obscene.text:
My dream has finally come true. I have been broken free of the shackles of the Wahl slice and dice. I have hated these those clippers since the first pair I owned back in '80s. They never stay sharp they never cut well. But enough about their crap.
I love love love these clippers. One pass did such an amazing job of a super close shave that I was blown away. Usually it takes a while to shave bald because there's always patches and stray hairs and you have to go over and over and over. That's not something you have to worry about with these. Seriously, I've been dreaming of these clippers for years I just couldn't find them. It was the glowing reviews from actual cosmetologists and barbers that caused me to select this product. I am so happy that professionals have started buying from Amazon so that I can learn from them through their reviews. like any of the barbers who have been in the business for 30 years, I will recommend these to everyone. They're sleek they're pretty, who knew you could have amazing function and form combined in one product. It keeps its charge for a super long time so you don't have to worry about interrupting a haircut. It really is a joy to use. it's probably a good thing I don't have dogs or they would have some pretty interesting haircuts. And I'm not going to say that I'm not looking at the cats out of the side of my eye though. That's right hair is hair. You can in fact cut animal hair with "people" clippers. Even if you don't know it they are often the same products. it's just like knowing what art supplies to buy from a hardware store instead of the art store.

I only have two complaints. One of the blade guards is melted and twisted so therefore completely unusable. My other complaint is that it did not come with a container. Though I must say the box it's shipped in is very nice for a cardboard box. As I mentioned I have those old horrible wahl clippers, so I threw them out kept the blade guards and my new wonderful clippers now live where the bastard clippers used to. The Wahl guards basically fit so I'm not too upset that one of the guards was wonky. Truthfully it's not even a length that I use.
top_five_star_obscene.title: I'm in love
